item.title: Balance of Terror
item.description:
This week, we take a look at one of the best episodes of Star Trek. Seriously, this is one you show someone who has never seen Star Trek before--it's that good. It's one we've wanted to talk about for a while and the time has finally come!

That episode is "Balance of Terror," from season one of The Original Series. We'll examine why this episode is so memorable--and how it made an impression on us at a young age.

Plus, lots of news including brand new Star Trek swag, news of a brand new Discovery comic from IDW, a special milestone for the Trek Geeks Podcast, and we'll announce the winners of two copies of Drastic Measures, signed by author Dayton Ward himself!

It's all in Episode 138 of Your Independent Star Trek Podcast, so beam aboard, Podfleet!

item.title: Say Something Nice
item.description:
There's always something to love in every episode of Trek and this week, we'll take a look at the episodes we like the least in a completely different light!

Introducing, "Say Something Nice!"

The rules are simple: take an episode you don't necessarily like and pick something you really enjoy about it. It could be a scene, or a production element, or a line, or even a performance. Anything that you think is a silver lining in an otherwise unlikable episode.

Joining us to find the best in these episodes is the most positive Star Trek fan we know: the Enterprise Extra! Jim Moorhouse from the Trek Ranks Podcast on The Tricorder Transmissions Network stops by to share his picks and talk about ours.

Which episodes make our list? Find out now!

Plus, we'll announce the four winners of the USS Enterprise NCC-1701-D from Eaglemoss Collections. Find out if you won and listen now!

item.title: Star Trek (2009)
item.description:
Few decisions in the Star Trek universe were more controversial than the announcement that there would be a new cast for movies featuring Captain Kirk, Mr. Spock, and the cast of Star Trek. This week, we'll look at that first film in what has become known as The Kelvin Timeline!

Directed by JJ Abrams, this movie went on to become a global success, bringing a new generation of fans into the Star Trek universe.

We'll discuss our reactions to the various announcements for this film--including the casting of the legendary Leonard Nimoy himself. We'll also look at the story and which elements we liked, as well as what things we might have liked to have seen changed.

item.title: Odo & Quark
item.description:
Of all the relationships in Star Trek, perhaps none is as layered and complex as the one between Odo and Quark.

On one side, you have the lawful Odo who is responsible for ensuring the safety of one of the Alpha Quadrant's most strategic locations. On the other, you have Ferengi bar owner Quark (he's *not* a barkeep) who seems to turn every situation into gray area with his dealings.

It's an unlikely friendship and we'll dive in on it this week!

Plus news, and an announcement from Dan and Bill on their new venture in this week's episode of Your Independent Star Trek Podcast!

item.title: The Enterprise
item.description:
It plays a central role in four television series and numerous films. We speak, of course, of the beloved Starship Enterprise and all her various incarnations!

Boldly going where none have gone before, the Enterprise helped us to discover the farthest reaches of the galaxy, and in this week's episode we'll take a look at this most storied ship in the Star Trek universe.

We'll look at the various incarnations and examine why this ship means so much to a lot of us.

Plus, lots of news and even some listener voice mails!

item.title: Discovering Trek: Summer Spectacular
item.description:
In this bonus Summer Spectacular episode of Discovering Trek, Dan & Bill take a look at some of the news and developments ahead of Star Trek: Discovery's second season. Plus, we'll review what we know about Discovery's sophomore season and what we think it might lead to!

=/\=

Music for Discovering Trek is provided by Five Year Mission. They're writing one song for each episode of The Original Series and their brand new album, Year 4, is available RIGHT NOW!

Find out more and download all their albums at FiveYearMission.net
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: Stump the Geek
item.description:
It is a contest as old as Star Trek itself. OK, well maybe it's not THAT old.

Once again, Dan Davidson will attempt to tame the behemoth that is Stump the Geek!

Dan will have to answer five questions of varying point values and he can't use any reference material. No websites. No books. No notes. He has to rely on the information in his walnut sized brain to answer these questions--and they are tougher than EVER!

After that, Dan will face the un-patented Double or Nothing Bonus Round! He'll wager everything in an attempt to be victorious!

Will he succeed? Check out this week's episode of Your Independent Star Trek Podcast to find out!

FIVE YEAR MISSION

We can't possibly thank FIVE YEAR MISSION enough. We are truly grateful to them for allowing us to use their music for every episode of Trek Geeks. You really should check them out–they're writing and recording one original song for each episode of the classic Star Trek series from the 1960's.

YEAR 4 IS HERE! Get the brand-new album from Five Year Mission RIGHT NOW at FiveYearMission.net

While you're at it, you should also pick up Year 1, Year 2, Year 3, The Trouble with Tribbles, and of course, Spock's Brain!

item.title: Inter Arma Enim Silent Leges
item.description:
Section 31.

The clandestine shadow organization appears from time to time to protect the affairs of the United Federation of Planets. It has existed since Starfleet's earliest beginnings, and now Luther Sloan has shown up to Deep Space Nine to recruit a new agent for a mission to Romulus...

Dr. Julian Bashir.

We'll look at this episode from DS9's final season and break it down for you! Plus, we'll have all the Discovery news from this past weekend's San Diego Comic Con.

It's all in episode 145 of Your Independent Star Trek Podcast, so beam on down!

item.title: Discovering Trek: Season 2 Trailer Reaction
item.description:
This past weekend at San Diego Comic-Con, the brand-new trailer for Season 2 of Star Trek: Discovery was released--and was it ever amazing!

We'll examine this preview of what's to come and tell you what we hope it all means as we kick off Discovering Trek's second season with our Trailer Reaction episode!

item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks: A Star Trek Podcast

item.title: A Piece of the Action
item.description:
It's one of the all-time great comedic episodes of Star Trek and the perfect send off in to Convention Week!

Tomorrow, we're on our way to Star Trek Las Vegas but today we're detouring at Sigma Iotia II to collect "A Piece of the Action."

One of the things that Star Trek has always done well is injecting levity after a few serious episodes and this is perhaps the best of them. We'll look at what makes this episode so much fun, as well as some of our favorite moments.

Plus, we'll give you all the latest breaking news on STLV. There's ONE DAY left before the convention kicks off, and new guests are still being added!

And don't forget about the amazing FanGeeks Party 2018 hosted by us on the one and only Las Vegas Strip! Our good friend, Lew Halboth from FanSets is going to stop by to tell you about all of the amazing pins that people will win at this year's party!

It's Episode 146 of Your Independent Star Trek Podcast, and it's a kick in the head, pally.

FIVE YEAR MISSION

We can't possibly thank FIVE YEAR MISSION enough. We are truly grateful to them for allowing us to use their music for every episode of Trek Geeks. You really should check them out–they're writing and recording one original song for each episode of the classic Star Trek series from the 1960's.

Please show them some support and download all their music!

item.title: STLV Recap
item.description:
...at Star Trek Las Vegas, DEFINITELY gets talked about on Trek Geeks!

There were a lot of developments at the biggest Trek Convention of the year, and we're going to talk about all the big news--including the gigantic surprise announcement: the return of Sir Patrick Stewart to Star Trek! We were there, and we'll tell you how it went down!

Joining us this week to talk about all the news (on all da StarTrekz) is the founder and editor of TrekNews.net himself, Brian Wilkins.

It was the weekend of a lifetime and we hope you'll join us in Episode 147 of Your Independent Star Trek Podcast.

FIVE YEAR MISSION We can't possibly thank FIVE YEAR MISSION enough. We are truly grateful to them for allowing us to use their music for every episode of Trek Geeks. item.title: Gul Dukat
item.description:
He is perhaps the greatest villain Star Trek has ever seen. Gul Dukat may only appear in 35 episodes of Star Trek: Deep Space Nine, but his impact is felt through the entire series.

We'll discuss the layers and complexities of Dukat and the remarkable performance by Marc Alaimo, plus we'll look at the various twists this character's development took over seven seasons.

Plus, we welcome FanSets as a sponsor to Trek Geeks--and they have a special discount just for Trek Geeks listeners for this week only!

item.title: See It or Skip It: DS9 S4
item.description:
This week, we return with yet another installment in our popular "See It or Skip It" series focusing on the fourth season of Star Trek: Deep Space Nine!

It's a season that begins with the introduction of Worf and ends with a revelation about Gowron with a lot of amazing episodes in between! Joining us to rate the season is our friend (and New York Times Best Selling Author) Dayton Ward! He'll guide us through the season and chime in with his own picks for this episode.

Plus, we have a special announcement regarding an incredible new project that we get to play a small part in and DON'T FORGET to listen for this week's special FanSets discount code just for listeners of the podcast!

It's all in Episode 149 of Your Independent Star Trek Podcast, so beam on in!

item.title: Star Trek's Finest Hour
item.description:
It is, perhaps, the greatest love story in Star Trek, and the finest hour of the franchise.

"The Visitor" tells the story of Benjamin Sisko's death and how it impacts his son Jake through the rest of his life. It is a powerful and emotional hour and we discuss it this week as a part of our 25th Anniversary salute to Star Trek: Deep Space Nine.

We'll look at why this episode means so much to so many, as well as the amazing performances from Avery Brooks and Tony Todd.

Plus, news and this week's FanSets special offer exclusively for Trek Geeks listeners in the 150th episode of Your Independent Star Trek Podcast!

item.title: Live from Granite State Comicon
item.description:
This past weekend, we hosted a Star Trek: Discovery panel at Granite State Comicon in Manchester, New Hampshire!

In this live recording of Trek Geeks, we'll talk about some of the key plot points of Season 1, and talk about what we might expect from Discovery's second season coming in early 2019!

Plus: AUDIENCE PARTICIPATION! It's our first-ever panel and live podcast--and we give away a TON of pins from our friends at FanSets, too!

FIVE YEAR MISSION!

item.title: Duet
item.description:
It is, perhaps, the best episode of Star Trek: Deep Space Nine's first season and it's among the best of the entire series.

"Duet" featured the story of a Cardassian war criminal to be put on trial by the Bajorans...or...does it?

We'll look at the Emmy worthy performances of Nana Visitor as well as guest star Harris Yulin in this compelling drama about hatred, forgiveness, and change.

Plus, we make a big announcement about Northeast Trek Con in Albany, NY on October 26-28! Plus, find out how YOU can win one of two weekend passes to this fantastic convention!

And don't think we forgot this week's discount code from FanSets! Oh, it's all in Episode 152 of Your Independent Star Trek Podcast and we hope you'll give it a listen!

FIVE YEAR MISSION

We truly grateful to our friends FIVE YEAR MISSION for allowing us to use their music for every episode of Trek Geeks. They're creating one song for every episode of TOS and their brand new album is amazing!

item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: Geek the Stump
item.description:
Throughout the decades, Star Trek fans have always tested each other at trivia. They challenge one another with seemingly impossible questions to celebrate the most obscure and celebrated aspects of Star Trek.

Yeah, that's not what's going to happen today.

This week, Bill will have to answer questions along the level of Teen Jeopardy in "Geek the Stump!" (Dan's turnabout response to our fan-favorite "Stump the Geek" episodes.)

Will Bill be able to answer the questions that a fifth grader who has only ever seen Trek ONCE could solve? We'll see! (It's a good thing he's on vacation because he may not want to come back after this performance!)

Enjoy the fun and the good times of Geek the Stump on this week's installment of Your Independent Star Trek Podcast!

item.title: See It or Skip It: DS9 Season 5
item.description:
We continue our celebration of Deep Space Nine's 25th Anniversary with yet another installment of See It or Skip It!

This time, we'll examine Season 5 of DS9 and joining us to referee is our dear friend (and one of our original fans) Hayley Stoddart from Trek.FM's Standard Orbit!

She'll have the unenviable task of reigning in the chaos of this episode and keeping us on task--not an easy job by any means!

Plus, we'll review the results of voting from Camp Khitomer--our Official Facebook Group--and see how their ratings measure up.

Don't forget to listen to hear about the NYCC exclusives now available and for your exclusive FanSets discount code for 15% off your entire order at FanSets.com!

Buckle up! This is going to be a fun two hours!

item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: Waltz
item.description:
He was the Prefect of Bajor and later the Leader of the Cardassian Union. On Stardate 51408.6, though, Gul Dukat held another notable title:

Prisoner.

...to the United Federation of Planets, and to the voices in his own head.

We'll take a look at this amazing sixth season episode of Star Trek: Deep Space Nine with a particular focus on the remarkable performances by Marc Alaimo and Avery Brooks.

Plus, we'll talk to two Star Trek legends: JG Hertzler and Robert O'Reilly about their upcoming appearance at the Northeast Trek Con at the Albany Capital Center in Albany, NY, October 28-28, 2018! Listen as we try to get a word in edgewise with these two hilarious gentlemen!

item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: Bride of Chaotica!
item.description:
Star Trek has always had some wonderfully fun episodes to break up the drama in a season, and Star Trek: Voyager is no exception!

Season 5's "Bride of Chaotica!" is a great homage to the sci-fi movie serials of the 30's and 40's like Buck Rogers and Flash Gordon, but this one has a decidedly Star Trek twist on it.

We'll talk about what makes this episode so great and why it's one you should watch again. It even gets Bill's thumbs up--and he'd never seen it before!

Plus, don't forget to listen for your special discount code from FanSets in this week's episode!

We're headed to Planet X in this week's edition of Your Independent Star Trek Podcast, so please join us!

BEGINNING THE TREK

Hear Bill and Dan guest along with Mission Log's John Champion on "Beginning the Trek" with Jessica Ray and Andy Goldberg!

http://beginningthetrek.com/podcast/bonus-wrap-up/

item.title: Take Me Out to the Holosuite
item.description:
It's the Great American Pastime in Space!

One of Deep Space Nine's most memorable outings has to be the seventh season episode, "Take Me Out to the Holosuite." Sisko accepts the challenge of a Vulcan nemesis in his favorite sport, putting the honor of the DS9 senior staff on the line!

It's an episode filled with humor and heart and perfect to discuss during this time of year when the baseball season comes to a close with the World Series.

We'll talk about what makes this episode so classic and about the one, true obsession of Benjamin Sisko.

This episode is recorded live at Northeast Trek Con in Albany, New York.

Buy me some peanuts and Cracker Jack--it's Your Independent Star Trek Podcast and it's time to PLAY BALL!

item.title: Podcasting 101 from Northeast Trek Con
item.description:
Dan was a bit under the weather last week, so we had to reschedule the episode we had planned for this week. We promise to bring it to you soon! As a result, we bring you the audio from our panel at Northeast Trek Con about podcasting and getting into this medium!

Joining us on this panel are Sue Kisenwether from Women at Warp, Brandon-Shea Mutala from Trek.FM's Warp Five and Melodic Treks podcasts, and moderating is Ken Tripp from Trek.FM's Standard Orbit!

item.title: In the Pale Moonlight
item.description:
It is a hour of Star Trek that is filled with compromise after disastrous compromise, and hanging in the balance is Benjamin Sisko's morality and self respect.

Starfleet is losing the war against the Dominion and they need the Romulans in the fight, and Sisko does what he never expected he'd have to.

We'll look at this incredible Deep Space Nine episode and discuss how Sisko has the weight of the Alpha Quadrant on his shoulders and humanity hangs in the balance.

This episode of Trek Geeks is no FAAAAAAAAAAKE and we hope you'll join us!

item.title: William Sadler
item.description:
You've seen him in the films Die Hard 2, Iron Man 3, Bill and Ted's Bogus Journey, and in television shows like Roswell, The Pacific, and Power.

But you've also seen him as the enigmatic Luther Sloan--Director of Section 31 in Star Trek: Deep Space Nine!

He's the incomparable William Sadler and, this week, he joins us on Your Independent Star Trek Podcast to chat about his career and playing the mysterious Sloan! It's a conversation you won't want to miss!

Plus, we've got some big news from FanSets--including a pin which will only be available on TrekGeeks.com in early 2019!

item.title: See It or Skip It: DS9 S6
item.description:
This week, we return with the highly requested See It or Skip It series, this time focusing on the penultimate season of Star Trek: Deep Space Nine!

We'll go through the entire season telling you which episodes you are definite must-sees, and which ones you could skip if you wanted to. Maybe.

Joining us in the fun is our good friend (and Camp Khitomer admin) Dan Garcia! He'll guide us through the entire season and even chime in with rankings of his own.

Plus, don't forget that there's another exclusive FanSets discount code just for Trek Geeks listeners!

Our Deep Space Nine 25th Anniversary is quickly drawing to a close on Your Independent Star Trek Podcast, so join us, won't you?

item.title: See It or Skip It: DS9 S7
item.description:
All good things must come to an end, and so it is with Star Trek: Deep Space Nine. You all made the Nice List this year, so Santa has brought you a special present!

It's our final installment in the See It or Skip It for DS9 and our last new episode of 2018! Joining us for this next installment is our dear friend, Norman Lao from ZocaloCast: A Babylon 5 VidCast!

You last heard Norman on the 100th Episode of Trek Geeks where we gave him the reins of the show, and he's back again to guide us through the chaos of our picks!

item.title: 2018 Outtakes Special, Part I
item.description:
The New Year is only a couple of weeks old and we thought we'd put together some special bonus content in the form of outtakes before we return with an all-new episode in a couple of weeks!

Many of you know that following the end theme of every podcast episode, there is an outtake–something that we talked about either immediately before or after recording that week's show.

Some of you have heard them, and some of you haven't, so we assembled a collection of all our clips from 2018 in not one, but TWO Supplemental Episodes of Trek Geeks! We had such great response to last year's Outtakes Special, that we doubled up on it this year, and we hope you enjoy it.

We hope that you all have the happiest of holidays and a safe and Happy New Year!

item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: Mission Briefing
item.description:
This week, we return from our extended holiday break to find that a LOT of news happened in the Star Trek universe while we were away!

Another new Star Trek series...

A possible shelving of Star Trek 4...

Lots of interviews and comments from Alex Kurtzman...

...plus, we'll tell you about our BRAND NEW podcast network!

Oh, and did we mention that we have an gorgeous exclusive pin from FanSets that we have for sale on the Trek Geeks website RIGHT NOW?!

News and more, in this week's installment of your Flagship Podcast on the Trek Geeks Network!

item.title: Religion in DS9
item.description:
This week, we're excited to welcome our friends from Trek.FM's Standard Orbit podcast to the Flagship of the Trek Geeks Podcast Network!

Join us and our friends Hayley Stoddart, Ken Tripp, and Zach Moore for an in-depth discussion on religion in the second Star Trek spin off series, Deep Space Nine. We'll discuss the introduction of a spiritual and religious people and the obvious conflict it created in the minds of some viewers.

Plus, we'll examine facets of of the Bajoran religion like the Kai, the Orbs, and the Emissary to the Prophets!

If you missed Part 1 of this conversation on God-like beings in TOS, be sure to head on over to Trek.FM and catch the first installment of this podcast crossover event! Click here to get that episode: Standard Orbit #255

item.title: Say Something Nice, Part Deux
item.description:
This week, we revisit some not-so-great episodes of Star Trek with a dear friend!

Every episode of Star Trek is someone's favorite and also someone's least favorite. We're going to take some episodes that we don't like as much and try to find something nice to say about them.

Joining us to mine something wonderful out of the mediocrity will be our friend, Michele Specht, from Star Trek Continues!

Plus, we'll have an announcement about a brand new podcast coming to the Trek Geeks Network later this year AND your exclusive FanSets discount code!

It's all in Episode 168 of The Biggest Little Show This Side of the Alpha Quadrant!

item.title: The Killing Game
item.description:
Back in October of 2018, we met our new friend Emilie at Northeast TrekCon in Albany, NY. We asked her to tell us her favorite Voyager episode and she didn't wait very long to tell us it was the two-part epic, "The Killing Game," from Star Trek: Voyager's fourth season.

We told her that we'd cover that episode just for her and that's what we're doing this week!

Then, we gave her homework. We asked Emilie to leave us a voicemail telling us WHY The Killing Game was her favorite Voyager episode.

So, this week, hear in her own words why this episode is so great, and then you'll hear us break it down--and, is there ever a lot to talk about! Holograms, Hirogen, and...Klingons?! Yep---KLINGONS!

Plus, news and your exclusive FanSets discount checkout code in this week's Flagship of the Trek Geeks Podcast Network!

item.title: Our Favorite Collectibles
item.description:
Collectibles.

It might be a fancy word for "stuff you buy," but these things mean something to us as Star Trek fans. It gives us a way to connect to this universe that we all love so much.

This week, we'll look at some of our favorite products from over the years. From things we had as kids, to things we WISH we had, to things we've collected as adults, and even those items we STILL want to this day!

It's a trip down memory lane this week on the Flagship of the Trek Geeks Network!

Plus news and your exclusive FanSets discount code in episode 170!

item.title: Carbon Creek
item.description:
April 5, 2063.

Bozeman, Montana.

First Contact between Humans and the visitors from Vulcan.

...or...was it?

Turns out in 1957, the initial contact we never knew about occurred in Pennsylvania when a Vulcan ship crash landed in the woods outside a small town in the eastern United States.

This week, we'll take a look at Carbon Creek from Star Trek: Enterprise's second season. T'Pol's second foremother was part of a group of Vulcans stranded in a Pennsylvania mining town in 1957. We'll see how they assimilated into life in a small town filled with lots of humans.

We'll discuss what makes this episode so great and why we love it.

Plus, don't forget this week's exclusive FanSets discount code in this week's installment of The Biggest Little Show This Side of the Alpha Quadrant!

item.title: The Women of Star Trek
item.description:
One of the reasons we love Star Trek is because of its incredible and amazingly strong women that have been a part of the franchise since the very beginning.

Characters. Actors. Writers. Producers. Directors. Artists.

They've all contributed to a rich tapestry for more than half a century and Star Trek wouldn't be what it is today without every single one of them.

This week, we'll look at the characters that have helped shape this franchise at every turn. From Lieutenant Uhura to Michael Burnham, we'll discuss the characters that we feel are among the most ground breaking and those that have come to mean a lot to us.

Obviously, there's no way we could discuss them all in a single Trek Geeks episode, so we'll spend the next year celebrating the contributions of women in Star Trek in every capacity.

Plus, we'll have a special bonus segment with our dear friend, Lew Halboth, from FanSets to announce their brand new Women of Star Trek master set which will debut this summer at STLV---and how you could help determine future pins in this series!

item.title: How to Be a Star Trek Fan
item.description:
Social Media.

It brings out the best and worst in people. This can be especially true of Star Trek fandom. The loudest voices are a small minority trying to impact the fandom of others.

That's why we're here!

We'll tell you about why Star Trek fandom is so great and how to enjoy this thing we all love even more. We aren't doing this alone, though.

We put out the call to our Official Facebook Group, Camp Khitomer, for help! You'll get to hear from some of the Campers on what Star Trek means to them, and why fandom is so important.

item.title: Devil in the Dark
item.description:
Essential Star Trek.

Almost everyone has their own definition, but almost every Trek fan believes that this episode should be on that list--and we agree completely.

"The Devil in the Dark" tells a story that speaks to the very heart of Star Trek and was some of the best science fiction produced for television then or now.

We'll discuss why this episode is so truly amazing and why it still is one of Trek's best outings and why it has stood the test of time.

Plus, we've got news and your exclusive FanSets discount code this week on The Biggest Little Show This Side of the Alpha Quadrant!

item.title: What You Leave Behind
item.description:
It's the end of an amazing Trek.

Deep Space Nine wrapped shooting 20 years ago this week and it's final episode, "What You Leave Behind," wraps up a nine part finale and a multi-season arc that changed Star Trek forever!

Bill and Dan will look at this last episode of DS9 and examine what makes this episode so special--from where the characters end up at the end to the end of Benjamin Sisko's journey.

Plus, this news and this week's exclusive FanSets discount code on The Flagship of the Trek Geeks Podcast Network!

item.title: The Wrath of Khan
item.description:
It is the Star Trek film to which all others are invariably compared at some point or another.

Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan breathed new life into the Star Trek franchise in the summer of 1982, and began a string of successful films which eventually resulted in Trek returning to television.

We'll discuss why we love this movie and what makes it the greatest of all time. Everything from favorite scenes to the captivating performance of the late Ricardo Montalban.

Plus, news and this week's exclusive FanSets discount offer code on the Flagship of the Trek Geeks Podcast Network!

item.title: Bonus Episode: Mary Chieffo
item.description:
This week, we bring you a special bonus episode of Discovering Trek!

CHANCELLOR OF THE KLINGON EMPIRE!

In this very special bonus episode, Dan & Bill are joined by "The Mother of all Klingons," "The Chancellor of the Klingon Empire," the honorable L'Rell of House Mokai!! That's right, the one and only Mary Chieffo stops by to talk about her incredible experience playing this amazing character on Star Trek: Discovery!

The Trek Geeks were honored and humbled to have Mary join them to discuss what it was like to prepare for this role as well as how much she loves to engage with fans. It is a wonderful discussion that you do not want to miss!

QAPLA'!!

item.title: Do You Wanna Build a Theme Park?
item.description:
This week, Dan is on vacation in Walt Disney World in Orlando, Florida, and it seemed like a good time to discuss this particular topic!

Last month on our Patreon Subscribers Feed, we talked about this topic in one of our Carpool KHAAANN-versations: one topic discussed in the time that it takes us to get to work commuting down Route 3 between Southern New Hampshire and Northern Massachusetts.

We'll discuss what attractions we might like to see, as well as what components we think a Star Trek themed park should have!

Plus, don't forget that we have news and this week's exclusive FanSets discount code on The Flagship!

item.description:
We are proud to introduce Trek Geeks Game Night--the Star Trek Adventures game play podcast!

Meet the crew of the USS Hathaway, the ship exploring the galaxy and boldly going in search of strange new worlds, new lifeforms, and new civilizations.

They'll introduce themselves in this special audio introduction and Game Master, Dan Garcia, will introduce their mission!

See all the latest Trek Geeks Game Night episodes on our YouTube channel YouTube.com/TrekGeeks and subscribe today!

item.title: The Inner Light
item.description:
It's widely regarded as one of the best hours of Star Trek: The Next Generation, and possibly of the entire franchise.

"The Inner Light" changed Jean-Luc Picard and viewers forever with it's poignant story about a civilization wiped out by its own star going nova a thousand years before.

Picard lives a lifetime in the course of twenty five minutes in an episode that tugs at your heartstrings. We'll discuss what makes this episode so great and why we love it.

Plus, your weekly FanSets discount code and news from the Star Trek universe on the Flagship of the Trek Geeks Podcast Network!

item.title: Available Light
item.description:
It's the first Star Trek: The Next Generation novel in nearly two years and it picks right up with the fallout from the exposure of Section 31!

"Section 31, the covert organization which has operated without accountability in the shadows for more than two centuries, has been exposed. Throughout the Federation, the rogue group's agents and leaders are being taken into custody as the sheer scope of its misdeeds comes to light. Now Starfleet Command must decide the consequences for numerous officers caught up in the scandal—including Admirals William Ross, Edward Jellico, Alynna Nechayev, and Captain Jean-Luc Picard who, along with many others, are implicated in the forced removal of a Federation president."

Plus, the Enterprise-E, still in the Odyssean Pass, has encountered a derelict ship---or is it?

There's a lot going on in this novel, and we'll be joined by author Dayton Ward to discuss all the details!

Plus news and a BIG announcement on the FanSets party in this week's episode of The Biggest Little Show This Side of the Alpha Quadrant!

item.title: The Voyage Home
item.description:
It's one of the greatest Star Trek movies of all time, and it was one that non-fans loved as much as some of Trek's most ardent supporters.

Star Trek IV: The Voyage Home debuted in 1986 and found a huge mainstream audience with its wit, humor, and awareness. Sadly, today this film seems more relevant than ever.

We'll look at some of the best moments of this film and discuss why it's so important in the pantheon of Star Trek films.

Plus, we'll have your weekly discount code from our friends at FanSets, as well as details on Trekonderoga this coming August at the Star Trek Original Series Set Tour.

item.title: The People Who Made TOS Work
item.description:
No one does it alone.

Not even "The Great Bird of the Galaxy."

The idea was Gene Roddenberry's, but it took an amazing group of people to help make it a reality. This week, to commemorate the 50th anniversary of Turnabout Intruder--the last episode of The Original Series--we look at The People Who Made TOS Work to shed some light on some names you may or may not know, and why their contributions to Star Trek were essential.

Joining us this week to look at some of the amazing contributors to Star Trek is our good friend Ken Tripp from Trek.FM's Standard Orbit podcast!

item.description:
This week, we offer another in our series on the "Women of Star Trek!"

One of the newest members of that group is the central figure in Star Trek: Discovery--the character of Michael Burnham. We'll take a look at this character and why she's so important, not just in Trek but in this day and age.

Plus, we're joined by Andy Fark of Five Year Mission for a special announcement!

item.title: Worst Case Scenario
item.description:
Star Trek has had a lot of holodeck stories where a malfunction has posed danger to the crew, but the concept rarely gets executed better than Voyager's third season episode, "Worst Case Scenario."

A tactical training program written by Tuvok becomes entertainment for the crew, and trying to write an ending executes potentially deadly subroutines left behind by Seska.

We'll discuss this great take on the holodeck and what makes this episode fun to watch, as well as some of the questions it poses.

Plus, find out how YOU can win tickets to Trekonderoga this August and get your exclusive FanSets discount code!

item.title: Special Announcement
item.description:
This week, we are excited and proud to welcome Barry DeFord and Shashank Avvaru to Trek Geeks to officially announce the Trek Geeks Podcast Network as the new home of PoliTreks: A Star Trek Podcast!

PoliTreks! Barry DeFord and Shashank Avvaru talk politics, religion, and society around the replicator. Along with special guests, we choose to Boldly Go straight into the social, political, and sometimes spiritual underpinnings at the core of Star Trek. In this podcast, we aim to seek out new perspectives, and discover the ways Trek - past and present - can help us better understand the world as it is, and us as we are. We travel at warp speed through worlds, timelines, and dimensions with one mission: make sense of our reality!

PoliTreks can be found where ever you download podcasts and also at PoliTreksPodcast.com!

item.description:
This week, while Dan and Bill are on vacation, we bring you one of our Carpool Conversations originally recorded for our Patreon subscribers.

Star Trek: Picard promises a different look at Jean-Luc Picard at this phase of his life, and that got us to thinking about which other Star Trek characters we'd like to see examined in their own theoretical spin-off series!

item.title: Star Trek Universe
item.description:
San Diego Comic-Con is one of the biggest fandom conventions on the planet, and this weekend some of the biggest news was all Star Trek related!

Three different Star Trek series were represented in a giant panel named, "Star Trek Universe," and there were surprises aplenty!

We'll review the latest details on "Star Trek: Discovery," chat about the newly-introduced characters of the second animated series in Star Trek history, "Star Trek: Lower Decks," and we'll react to the thoroughly jaw-dropping reveals in the "Star Trek: Picard" panel!

Joining us to break it all down is Barry DeFord--co-host of "PoliTreks" and the host of the forthcoming "Trek Geeks: Picard LIVE!" debuting later this year!

There's a lot to talk about, and we're excited to bring it to you!

Plus, don't forget about your exclusive FanSets discount code in this latest episode of the Flagship of the Trek Geeks Podcast Network!

item.title: The Gamesters of Triskelion
item.description:
What better way to kick off the week of STLV than to focus on some high stakes gambling!

This week we look at the classic TOS episode, The Gamesters of Triskelion! Drill Thralls, pain collars, Providers, and lots of wagering highlight this second season installment. It's an episode we've been watching as kids, but in this episode of The Biggest Little Show, we'll examine what makes this episode classic, flaws and all.

Plus, don't forget about your exclusive FanSets discount code on the Flagship of the Trek Geeks Podcast Network!

item.title: The State of Star Trek
item.description:
We had an amazing time at Star Trek Las Vegas and the biggest Trek convention on the planet. We saw a lot of old friends, made some new ones, and experienced a great celebration of this franchise we all love so deeply.

This week, we'll look at the state of the Star Trek franchise and discuss everything the states of fandom, the convention, and even the franchise itself!

We'll also announce the winners of tickets to Trekonderoga at the Star Trek Original Series Set Tour in Ticonderoga, NY on August 23-25, 2019!

Plus, your exclusive FanSets discount code in this week's episode of The Biggest Little Show This Side of the Alpha Quadrant!

FIVE YEAR MISSION

Every episode, you hear the music of Five Year Mission on the Trek Geeks Podcast and we are so grateful to them for allowing us to use their songs.

You know they have Year 1, Year 2, Year 3, and now the Year 4 album. You know they even have the Spock's Brain and The Trouble With Tribbles Albums. But get ready as they prepare the Five Year Mission Podcast debuting on the Trek Geeks Network in 2019!

That means you need to get ready! Go to FiveYearMission.net RIGHT NOW and start by ordering copies of all their CDs--because physical media is key--and start listening! We just KNOW you'll become huge fans of the band and their music!

item.title: Majel Barrett Roddenberry
item.description:
Our Women of Trek spotlight series continues this week as we take a look at one of Star Trek's original contributors.

She was an absolute force of nature, and in this episode we'll examine the roles played by Majel Barrett Roddenberry in front of the camera, behind the scenes, and in fandom itself.

Number One, Christine Chapel, Lwaxana Troi...even the Enterprise Computer, Majel left an indelible mark on the franchise and created characters that we continue to talk about to this day.

But there was far more than that. Majel was an Ambassador of Trek, especially in the years after Gene passed, and we'll examine that aspect of her life as well, and consider her legacy.

Plus, we'll have a full length preview of the brand new Five Year Mission Podcast at the end of this episode and don't forget about your exclusive FanSets discount code!

FIVE YEAR MISSION

Speaking of 5YM, every week you hear the music of Five Year Mission on the Trek Geeks Podcast and we are so grateful to them for allowing us to use their songs.

item.title: Trekonderoga
item.description:
We had the incredible good fortune to attend this year's Trekonderoga convention in Ticonderoga, New York--home of the Star Trek Original Series Set Tour!

Featuring Star Trek luminaries like Terry Farrell, Robin Curtis, Ethan Peck, JG Hertzler, and David Frankham, Trekonderoga had a fantastic track of Star Trek content.

At the heart of it, the amazing recreation of the sets from Star Trek--lovingly and meticulously recreated by James Cawley. It was an immersive experience that made you feel like you were really there.

We'll discuss our weekend at this intimate convention and why we'll be back next year! Plus, details about our conversations with Ethan Peck, and details on our standing room only panel!

Plus, get your exclusive FanSets discount code and the news from TrekNews.net on the Flagship of the Trek Geeks Podcast Network!

FIVE YEAR MISSION

item.title: Face of the Enemy
item.description:
This week, we examine what is quite possibly the best Deanna Troi episode in all of Star Trek: The Next Generation!

Romulans...intrigue...defectors...kidnapping...and a surgically altered Deanna?! "Face of the Enemy" debuted in 1993 and it instantly became a favorite of fans everywhere.

Counselor Troi is kidnapped by Romulans and her appearance is changed so she can assume the identity of a Tal'Shiar operative to help dissidents defect to the Federation.

Meanwhile, a traitor to Starfleet returns and brings Jean-Luc Picard a message from Ambassador Spock and the Enterprise becomes part of this crucial mission.

We'll discuss it all in this episode of Trek Geeks! From the development created for Deanna Troi to the amazing performance by Marina Sirtis! We love this episode, and we'll tell you why!

Plus, we'll have news and your weekly FanSets discount code!

item.title: What I've Learned From Saru
item.description:
PARENTAL ADVISORY: The following episode contains adult themes, adult language, and a frank discussion on child abuse and its aftermath. PARENTAL DISCRETION IS STRONGLY ADVISED.

Earlier in 2019, Bill wrote a column for the Official Star Trek Website titled, "What I've Learned From Saru." In it, he discussed what the Kelpien from Star Trek: Discovery has taught him in his journey living with anxiety.

As he continued to examine the character, he found that he identified with Saru in more ways than he thought.

This episode contains very personal and disturbing revelations about Bill's childhood and, as a result, we are cautioning parents that this installment may not be suitable for younger listeners.

FIVE YEAR MISSION Episode 2 of Five Year Mission: The Podcast is available now and you'll hear Mike and Fark have a discussion on "Is It REEEEEAL Or Is It FAAAAAAAAKE?"

item.title: Aron Eisenberg 1969-2019
item.description:
This week, we pause to reflect on the sudden passing of Star Trek: Deep Space Nine's Aron Eisenberg who died this past weekend at the age of 50.

Special thanks to Mike Rittenhouse of Five Year Mission for his acoustic rendition of the Deep Space Nine theme.
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: The Corbomite Manuever
item.description:
It was the third hour of Star Trek ever produced, and it's considered among the best of The Original Series.

"The Corbomite Manuever" was also the episode that launched Bill's Star Trek fandom in the mid-1970's as the first episode he ever saw.

This week, we'll examine this classic episode of Star Trek and discuss why it still means so much today. Plus, we'll talk about the impact this episode had on us as children and what it means to us today.

Plus news and your weekly FanSets discount code on the Flagship of the Trek Geeks Podcast Network!

FIVE YEAR MISSION

item.title: Artificial Intelligence in Star Trek
item.description:
It seems that technology touches our everyday lives, and Star Trek seems to have inspired it all!

From smart phones to digital assistants, Trek showed us that artificial intelligence would become part of our daily routines, and how right it was.

We'll examine everything from computers to androids and photonic beings! Joining us in this special roundtable discussion are two of our Patreon Producers, Jamie Rogers and Ken Byrd!

It's a discussion that our favorite Vulcan might call...fascinating!

Plus, news and your weekly FanSets discount code on The Flagship!

item.title: The Andorian Incident
item.description:
A Vulcan Monastery on a planet that's not on the star charts?

That's what the crew of the NX-01 decides to visit and they soon discover that they're not alone.

This week, we look at the Season 1 episode, "The Andorian Incident" from Star Trek: Enterprise! Airing on Halloween Night 2001, this episode brought back an alien species that we hadn't seen since The Animated Series in the 1970's--and they cast the incredible Jeffrey Combs (Weyoun, DS9) as the perfect foil for Captain Jonathan Archer!

We'll dive in on this episode and talk about why the Vulcans are such jerks, and why this episode is fantastic despite that.

Plus, we'll announce the winner of the FanSets AI pins and have your special discount code for fansets.com--all on The Flagship!

FIVE YEAR MISSION

item.title: Kira Nerys
item.description:
Our Women of Star Trek spotlight series continues with a character that broke the mold for female characters in Trek!

She's the First Officer of space station Deep Space Nine and the Bajoran military liaison to Starfleet. A former terrorist, we meet her as part of the mainstream government that settled in after the Cardassian occupation of Bajor.

This week, we examine the character of Kira Nerys and her journey across the seven seasons of Deep Space Nine. We'll discuss the arc of her character's development and what makes her one of the most important Women of Trek.

Plus, our friends at FanSets have a truly amazing giveaway for one lucky Trek Geeks listener. Find out how you can win one of their framed Women of Star Trek master sets in this week's episode!

item.title: The Completist
item.description:
There are approximately 750 hours of Star Trek and fans who have seen every episode and movie are typically referred to as "completists."

Until recently, Bill had not been part of that club. He hadn't seen most of Star Trek: Voyager and had a goal of finishing that by the 25th anniversary of the series in 2020.

Just a couple of weeks ago he completed the series, earning him his completist badge and we figured it would be a great time to hear his thoughts on the fourth live action Star Trek series.

Joining us to celebrate the milestone is our good friend John Krikorian from the Trek Profiles podcast--who is also in the midst of trying to finish Voyager for the first time! We'll discuss Bill's reactions to the series and he may even have to answer a lightning round of questions!

Plus, your FanSets discount checkout code this week on The Flagship!

item.title: Peak Performance
item.description:
In the wake of the newly realized Borg threat, Starfleet has begun war games to test the strategy and the battle readiness of the crew and the USS Enterprise is front and center.

Their opponent is the derelict starship USS Hathaway that shouldn't have a chance--but with Will Riker in command, anything is possible!

"Peak Performance" is the last totally original episode in Season 2 of Star Trek: The Next Generation before the 1989 Writers Strike, and it's a fun watch. Sure, this episode has a bunch of issues, but it doesn't take away from how truly fantastic it is, and we'll talk about why!

Joining us to break down this episode is our great friend Dan Garcia, the host of Trek Geeks Game Night on our YouTube channel!

Plus, we'll giveaway the framed FanSets Women of Trek set to one lucky listener on this week's episode of The Flagship!

item.title: The Wire
item.description:
Dr. Julian Bashir has one of his toughest patients yet.

At lunch with plain, simple Garak one afternoon, the Cardassian tailor begins to experience a severe headache. It turns out that a device in Garak's head meant to help him survive torture, is now helping him survive his exile on Deep Space Nine.

Now he's addicted to it, and the device is starting to break down after nearly two years of constant use, and Garak's life hangs in the balance.

We'll look at this amazing second season entry for Deep Space Nine and the standout performances by both past Trek Geeks guest Andy Robinson and Alexander Siddig.

Plus, your weekly FanSets discount code on the Flagship of the Trek Geeks Podcast Network!

item.title: The 200th Episode
item.description:
This week, we celebrate a milestone in the history of the Trek Geeks Podcast: the release of our 200th Episode!

It's been a long road, getting from there to here--and we've had nothing but fun along the way! Join the hosts for this special episode, Barry DeFord and Shashank Avvaru from PoliTreks as they discuss the history of Trek Geeks, chat with special guests, and remind Bill and Dan exactly how much they've forgotten about their own podcast!

Plus, there's an extra special discount from FanSets this week--one you definitely won't want to miss!

Bill and Dan also wish to offer their thanks to their wives Kelly Smith and Susan Davidson, to Barry and Shashank for taking on the hosting duties of this epic episode, to their supporters on Patreon, and to everyone that has downloaded and listened to Trek Geeks over the last five years. We truly wouldn't be here without you and we're so incredibly grateful.

FIVE YEAR MISSION

We'd also like to thank Five Year Mission for their support and for saying "yes" to an email request they received from two geeks in New Hampshire about using their music in a podcast.

item.description: This week, we return to our microphones to pause and reflect on the passing of Star Trek: Deep Space Nine's René Auberjonois who died last month at the age of 79.

item.title: Voyager 25
item.description:
This week, we return after our holiday hiatus with a look at Star Trek: Voyager!

As we prepare to celebrate Voyager's 25th Anniversary all year long, we'll look at Voyager's premiere back in 1995 and the hope we had for the series and how our appreciation has evolved over the last quarter century.

Plus, we'll look at Voyager's characters and how they were able to break new ground while also creating stories that engaged and made us think.

Plus news, and your weekly FanSets discount on The Flagship!

FIVE YEAR MISSION

We'd also like to thank Five Year Mission for their support and for saying "yes" to an email request they received from two geeks in New Hampshire about using their music in a podcast.

item.title: Gone Hollywood
item.description:
Photo Credit: @StarfleetMom

Recently, Bill and Dan made the trip out to Hollywood, California to attend the red carpet premiere for Star Trek: Picard--the latest series in the Star Trek universe starring none other than Sir Patrick Stewart.

This week, they'll talk about their trip to the La-La Land from dinner at Hollywood's oldest restaurant, to a special VIP tour of Paramount Pictures, all the way through the screening of the first three episodes of Picard!

It was an experience of a lifetime and we'll tell you all about it this week on The Flagship of the Trek Geeks Podcast Network!

item.title: See It or Skip It: Voyager Season 2
item.description:
It's been fourteen months since we last went through a season of Star Trek with our votes to "See It or Skip It," and this week the fan favorite series returns!

What better way to continue our year long celebration of Voyager's 25th Anniversary than to vote on the seasons!

This week we're joined by Guest Referee, Debbie Moltisanti to go through Voyager's second season to pass judgment on each and every episode. Plus, we'll tell you where the members of our Official Facebook Group, Camp Khitomer, come down on each episode, too!

item.title: Paradise
item.description:
Trapped on a planet where electronics and devices are rendered inoperable, Sisko and O'Brien are taken in by a group of human colonists in the Gamma Quadrant--but what are they doing there?

All is not as it appears as we look at Deep Space Nine's "Paradise" from Season 2.

We'll examine the things that work (or don't) in this episode and how it might be even more relevant today than it was in 1994.

Plus, when we tell you that there's a lot of news from FanSets, that's almost not doing it justice. We have a lot to announce and, well...let's just say you're going to save some quatloos in the process.

It's all in this week's episode of The Flagship, so join us, won't you?

item.title: The Romulans
item.description:
They're one of the most feared and most deceitful adversaries we've ever seen.

From their first appearance in Balance of Terror, the Romulans have been a constant threat to Starfleet and the security of the United Federation of Planets.

They've been featured pretty heavily in Star Trek: Picard, so we decided this would be a great time to look at this race. We'll examine the Romulan society and the history of the Romulans, plus we'll take a look at some of our standout episodes and some of our favorite Romulans ever.

Plus news and your weekly FanSets discount code on The Flagship of the Trek Geeks Podcast Network---JOLAN TRU!

item.title: Blink of an Eye
item.description:
Our year-long celebration of Star Trek: Voyager's 25th Anniversary continues and, this week, we look at one of Voyager's best.

"Blink of an Eye" from Season 6 puts the crew in a situation we've never seen: stuck in the orbit of a planet where time passes much faster. They witness centuries of development of this planet and shape much of the society's progress.

We'll discuss what makes this such an amazing episode of Star Trek, along with standout performances and the questions it makes us ask.

Plus news and your weekly FanSets discount code on The Flagship!

FIVE YEAR MISSION

Every note of music you hear on Trek Geeks is the amazing work of the band Five Year Mission. They're writing one song for each episode of Star Trek--and we are HUGE fans!

They even have a rather eponymously named podcast here on the Trek Geeks Network! This week, their kids take to the mic to tell us what they've learned about Star Trek from dudes in a Star Trek band! Subscribe now on Apple Podcasts, Google Podcasts, Spotify, and wherever you download podcasts!

5YM, of course, is every note of music you hear on Rewind, and we couldn't be more grateful. item.title: Embracing the Suck: Threshold
item.description:
Last week we looked at one of Star Trek: Voyager's best episodes and now the pendulum must swing the other direction.

It's probably one of the most maligned Voyager episodes of all time, and this week, we're going to look at it a little differently.

Join us as we kick off a new series which looks at some episodes that aren't as popular and finds a reason to love them! It's "Embracing the Suck," and in this episode we'll examine Voyager's "Threshold" in our first installment.

Even Brannon Braga calls it terrible, but we'll look at the reasons why you should love it and why it might not be as bad as nearly everyone thinks.

Plus news, an update on our first-ever live show, and your weekly FanSets discount on The Flagship!

item.title: BONUS: Distractions
item.description:
This is the audio-only version of the live stream to Facebook/YouTube on March 19, 2020.

There's a lot going on in the world, so let's get together and talk about…well…nothing! We could all use a distraction so we're going to do our best to provide one. We don't even know if we have a topic or a plan, but we'll give this a shot anyway!

Join Bill, Sara, Barry, and Dan for a discussion about whatever comes up to distract us from the news of the day!
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: Variations on a Theme
item.description:
Let's face it. Depending on where you live, you've probably been socially distancing in your house, trying to help flatten the curve of COVID-19. It's probably been at least a couple of weeks and you might be starting to get a little stir crazy.

That's where we come in!

We've all seen Star Trek a bunch, so we came up with some themed re-watches to help you watch the episodes a little differently and help pass the time!

Joining us is our great friend, Trek Geeks Producer, and Camp Khitomer Admin, Jackie Hackney and between the three of us, we'll have some great suggestions for you!

item.title: Chakotay
item.description:
As our year-long celebration of Star Trek: Voyager's 25th Anniversary continues, this week we examine a character that we've really grown to love.

As First Officer of the USS Voyager, he was responsible for executing the Captain's vision--but that wasn't how his path began in "Caretaker." A Maquis terrorist, he was thrust in to the role when stranded in the Delta Quadrant. Over time, he became indispensable to Captain Janeway and the epitome of what an outstanding Starfleet Officer should be.

Join us this week as we look at Chakotay on the Flagship of the Trek Geeks Podcast Network!

item.title: Say Something Nice, Part III
item.description:
During this time of global pandemic, we thought to ourselves, "What could we do to give people a fun distraction and make them forget about COVID 19 for a while?"

Then, we had the idea to take the normal episode of Trek Geeks we'd do and do it as a Live Stream! On Tuesday night, April 7, 2020, we did just that! We had a great time with everyone during "Say Something Nice, Part III," and we share the recording of that with you this week.

Bill and Dan will each pick five things to say nice things about and, this time, they may be part of Trek or they may not!

Plus some news and TWENTY PERCENT discount from FanSets this week on the Flagship!

item.title: Star Trek: Picard Season 1
item.description:
The return of Jean-Luc Picard to Star Trek was truly epic. The first season may have been only ten episodes, but it truly took viewers where no Star Trek had gone before.

It also marked the first Trek series since Trek Geeks began that Bill & Dan just got to watch and enjoy as fans.

For the first time, they'll talk about their reactions to the season--from the time it was announced at Star Trek: Las Vegas to the final moments of the season finale, they'll talk about it all.

Plus, one sentence reviews from our Official Facebook Group, Camp Khitomer and your weekly FanSets discount code on The Flagship!

item.title: Tearjerkers
item.description:
Over the decades, Star Trek has provided us with some incredible moments that have run the gamut of emotion.

In this week's episode, Bill and Dan will examine Trek's best Tearjerkers--those episodes that have provided moments so emotional that they've made us shed a tear or two...or a thousand.

Of course, this is by no means a definitive list. We want to know your picks for Trek's most emotional moments. Let us know!

Plus news, your weekly discount code from FanSets, and we welcome a brand new Star Trek licensee as a sponsor to the podcast!

item.title: Tuvix
item.description:
Star Trek has always given us things to think about and topics to examine and discuss, but few episodes have generated the type of reaction as the one we'll discuss this week.

We'll continue our salute to Star Trek: Voyager's 25th Anniversary this week as we look at the second season episode, "Tuvix." Part Tuvok, Part Neelix, and all the makings of a legendary hour of the franchise.

Did Captain Janeway do the right thing--and what did it cost her? We'll discuss that and more this week!

Plus news and a brand new discount from our friends at Science Division on the adoption of a new Tribble on The Flagship of the Trek Geeks Podcast Network, presented by FanSets!

item.title: Alexander Siddig
item.description:
For seven seasons, he played Dr. Julian Bashir on Star Trek: Deep Space Nine and, this week, actor Alexander Siddig sits down with Bill and Dan to chat about his time on Star Trek as well his experiences on other shows such as Game of Thrones and Gotham.

Plus, find out how he's passing the time during this period of self-quarantine--and what games he's fond of playing!

It's a truly delightful conversation and we hope you'll join us!

item.title: Strange Other Worlds
item.description:
Over the decades, Star Trek has become part of international pop culture--to the point where references and parodies turn up in other productions. They say imitation is the sincerest form of flattery and, if that's true, Star Trek is probably one of the most imitated and referenced shows in history.

This week, we'll take a look at some of our favorite occurrences of Star Trek popping up in other media. Joining us for the conversation is our dear friend and fellow podcaster, Hayley Stoddart!

Plus, get the details on Hayley's brand new podcast, SCIENCE STATION 2, coming to the Trek Geeks Network this year!

item.title: Lost Audio from Kingsland
item.description:
It's only taken us four years but this week on Trek Geeks, we'll right a grievous error.

If you recall our 100th episode, "A Journey to Future's Past, Part I: Beaming Aboard Star Trek Continues," we told the story of our trip Kingsland, Georgia and visit to sets of this legendary fan film series. We told you about the people we met and how our love for The Original Series had been rekindled. We brought you a story of family. It was the most ambitious episode we've ever done: an audio documentary in the style of National Public Radio, and it's become a favorite among Trek Geeks listeners. It was an episode we still get feedback about, and it even led to us being a finalist for the Parsec Awards.

It was our every intention to bring you part two of that story, and then CBS started making more Star Trek. They launched Discovery and we started a second podcast. Then we started a network. Now with eight Star Trek series in some form of production, we decided that we needed to bring you the conversations from Day 2 of our trip.

So, in this episode, you'll hear from Grant Imahara, Wyatt Lenhart, and our dear friend Kasey Shafsky. Plus, we'll also have some audio of our final walkthrough of the sets before we said goodbye to the Starship Enterprise for the last time.

Plus, find out about our charity livestream with Jonathan Frakes from Star Trek: The Next Generation coming up on June 11th! Get all the details!

item.title: Dr. Erin Teaches Science to Two Idiots
item.description:
What happens when you take someone who is an astrophysicist and consults on Star Trek and subject her to explaining the science behind things to...well...us?

We're not sure, either, but we're pretty sure that her head exploding might be a possible outcome!

You might have seen her at STLV or maybe even in her online series, "Dr. Erin Explains the Universe." This week, we welcome Dr. Erin MacDonald to the podcast to talk about the science of things in Star Trek and whether or not some of these things make sense.

Plus, we'll talk about the rescheduled dates for STLV and give you an update on our online fundraiser for Feeding America in this week's episode of The Flagship of the Trek Geeks Podcast Network!

item.title: Coda
item.description:
Star Trek has always told stories about the human condition and, this week, we look at an episode that truly explores "the final frontier."

"Coda" from the third season of Star Trek: Voyager is part time loop and part alien infiltration. In it we see Kathryn Janeway die over and over and over--and we learn things about her crew that we might not have learned otherwise. It is an amazing episode with layer upon layer of character building.

We'll dive in on this hugely underrated episode in this week's Trek Geeks and tell you why it's better than you probably think it is!

Plus news and discount codes from our friends at FanSets and Science Division!

item.title: Jonathan Frakes
item.description:
This week, we're incredibly proud to bring you the audio from our Feeding America Charity Livestream with Star Trek: The Next Generation's Jonathan Frakes!

We'll talk about his career, returning to the Captain's Chair as Captain William T. Riker, and we'll even put Jonathan in the hot seat and play a game of "This or That!"

It was an amazing night and we raised money to provide just over 56,000 meals to people in need! While our campaign is over, you can still help those in need by donating to Feeding America directly through their website.

item.title: To Be Continued...
item.description:
Some episodes of Star Trek are just so epic, they can't be contained to a mere hour of television.

The precedent started in 1966 with Star Trek's, "The Menagerie," and it's a format that occurred all the way up through Star Trek: Enterprise: the two-part episode!

Sometimes they're a cliffhanger to give us something to anticipate over the summer hiatus and, other times, they're stories that happen mid-season. In any case, there are things to love about all of them and we'll dive into some of that this week.

Joining us to talk about some legendary two-parters are Patreon producers Christina Werther and Chris Tribuzio! It's a fun discussion that we hope you'll enjoy. Plus, like the topic at hand, it takes more than an hour to talk about this fantastic topic!

Plus, since there's no possible way we could talk about them all, we want to know YOUR favorite two-parters! Let us know on social media!

FIVE YEAR MISSION

item.title: The Final Frontier
item.description:
It's cited by many fans as one of their least favorite Star Trek movies, but is it really that bad?

When Star Trek V: The Final Frontier hit theaters in 1989, it was on the heels of the wildly successful fourth movie that had a wide appeal. Expectations and anticipation were high, but the movie left fans feeling somewhat disappointed. We'll take a look at some of the challenges this movie had to overcome.

We'll also look closely at the things that work extremely well in this movie and why the movie might be a lot better than people think. Our feelings just might have changed about this movie in the 31 years since it was released, and we can't wait to tell you why.

Joining us to look at this movie is New York Times bestselling author and friend of the show, Dayton Ward! It's a conversation we hope you'll enjoy and, we promise, no fan dancing.

item.title: SPECIAL PREVIEW: Infinite Trek
item.description:
In this special full-length preview of Infinite Trek: Exploring the Star Trek Universe, hosts Aaron Harvey and Brandi Jackola introduce us to their brand new podcast on the Trek Geeks Podcast Network!

Infinite Trek will cover EVERYTHING Star Trek from animation to live action, comics to novels, from games to real life tech—if it's Star Trek or even Star Trek adjacent, it's here!

Our year-long celebration of Star Trek: Voyager's 25th Anniversary continues and, this week, we examine the character of Captain Kathryn Janeway!

We'll take a look at this unique and bold character in the Star Trek Universe and discuss why this Captain is among Starfleet's all-time best.

Joining us in the discussion is our dear friend, Amy Imhoff! You've probably seen her in panels at Star Trek Las Vegas or even read her writing about Star Trek, The X-Files and Battlestar Galactica. item.title: A Conversation with Mike McMahan
item.description:
It's the latest Star Trek series and the first animated series set in the Star Trek Universe in almost fifty years. In this bonus episode of Discovering Trek and Trek Geeks, Dan and Bill sit down to talk with Star Trek: Lower Decks creator and executive producer, Mike McMahan about the show, its comedy, the DNA it shares with The Animated Series, and how it's inspired fans already.

WE COVER LOWER DECKS LIKE NO ONE ELSE

Interested in great talk about Star Trek: Lower Decks? Look no further than the Trek Geeks Podcast Network! Discovering Trek breaks down every episode on Mondays with our own Lower Decks crew of Dan, Sara, Kasey and Bill. Plus, check out Infinite Trek on Tuesdays with Brandi Jackola and Aaron Harvey (co-author of Star Trek: The Official Guide to the Animated Series)!

No One Talks Trek Like We Do.

FIVE YEAR MISSION

Music for Discovering Trek and Trek Geeks is provided by Five Year Mission. They're writing one song for each episode of The Original Series! Plus, check out their podcast right here on the Trek Geeks Podcast Network!

Find out more and download all 6 of their current albums at FiveYearMission.net
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks: A Star Trek Podcast

item.title: Scorpion
item.description:
A cliffhanger.

A new crew member.

A crew member on her way out.

An old threat.

A new threat.

The third season cliffhanger and fourth season premiere covered a lot of ground and really changed the stakes for the crew of the USS Voyager. "Scorpion" added new elements that in a way that really turned the show on its ear by introducing an adversary that even the Borg feared!

This is definitely one of Voyager's best and we can't wait to talk about it as our celebration of #Voyager25 rolls on!

Plus, news and discount codes from FanSets and Science Division! It's all here on The Flagship.

No One Talks Trek Like We Do.

item.title: Seven of Nine
item.description:
As we continue our celebration of Star Trek: Voyager's 25th Anniversary, this week we take a look that a character that came on board almost mid-way through the series but very quickly became nothing short of legendary.

Born Annika Hansen, she was assimilated by the Borg at six years old. Eighteen years later, she would be separated from the Collective and become a valued member of the Starship Voyager's crew. This week, we look at the character of Seven of Nine!

This was a character that added some new qualities and different relationships for Voyager and, once again, gave us a character struggling with the concept of their own humanity--albeit in a very different way. We'll discuss why this character has become one we truly love and why we're very glad her story is far from over!

No One Talks Trek Like We Do.

item.title: Captains Courageous
item.description:
They've boldly gone and explored strange new worlds. They've made the decisions of life and death for those under their command. They've upheld the best traditions of Starfleet.

With the 54th anniversary of Star Trek being celebrated this week, Bill & Dan take a look at the central figures from most of the Star Trek series: the Captains.

From Captain Christopher Pike in "The Cage" to Acting Captain Saru in Star Trek: Discovery, we'll look at what makes these characters worthy of the Center Seat and why they embody the best of Starfleet.

BONUS PREVIEW!

This episode also contains a full-length preview of the introductory episode of Deep Space Pride: A Gay Star Trek Podcast, so be sure to keep listening after the end theme! You can find out more about Deep Space Pride by visiting deepspacepride.com and SUBSCRIBE NOW on Apple Podcasts!

item.title: If Memory Serves
item.description:
This week, our discussion on Christopher Pike, Vina, and the Talosians wraps up with a look at Star Trek: Discovery!

In Discovery's second season, we returned to Talos IV in an episode titled, "If Memory Serves." Michael Burnham needed the help of the Talosians to unlock Spock's memories and that wasn't all.

This episode saw a reunion of Pike and Vina 54 years after The Cage was produced, and we learned even more about the bond between these characters.

We'll talk about this continuation of their story and why it worked so very well. Plus news and special discounts from FanSets and Science Division in this week's episode of The Flagship!

item.title: The Augments Arc
item.description:
Season 4 of Star Trek: Enterprise had several small, multi-episode arcs which helped extend the story telling and the characters. This week, we look at the first of those arcs which saw the return of a legendary Trek actor!

In 2004, Brent Spiner returned to Star Trek on television to portray one of the ancestors of Data's creator, Arik Soong, who was pretty big on genetic engineering and it looks like his "kids"--the augments he'd raised from embryos--are on the verge of starting a war between Earth and the Klingons.

It's The Augments Arc this week on the Flagship of the Trek Geeks Podcast Network!

item.title: The Haunting of Deck Twelve
item.description:
Who doesn't love a Ghost Story?

The power is out ship-wide. There's a lantern. There's even snacks. It's the perfect setting for a scary tale!

Voyager Morale Officer Neelix spins a yarn for the Borg kids aboard the ship about The Haunting of Deck Twelve.

Bill and Dan will dive in on this penultimate episode of Voyager's sixth season and talk about what works and what doesn't. It's the perfect episode in advance of Halloween---or is it?

item.title: The Ship
item.description:
While on a survey mission in the Gamma Quadrant to establish a potential mining colony, Sisko and company come across a Jem'Hadar transport ship that has crash landed. This could be the most significant intelligence find for Starfleet in recent memory--but it's not that easy.

The Dominion want the ship back and it's the only thing keeping our crew safe. Tensions and stakes are incredibly high in "The Ship" from Season 5 of Star Trek: Deep Space Nine, and this week we dive in on this incredible episode!

item.description:

This episode has long been one of Bill's favorites, and even Future Bill from six hours from now, too. It's one of the Season 2 standouts--even for a bottle show--and it shows why Patrick Stewart was at the top of the Call Sheet.

It's an episode that Dan initially said to skip in the Season 2 See It or Skip It--Find out why we he's changed his vote on #TheFlagship!

item.title: Year of Hell
item.description:
After an unscheduled week off (sorry about that, everyone) we're back and we're continuing our celebration of #Voyager25 in a big way!

Airing in 1997, "Year of Hell" quickly established itself as one of Voyager's best outings and a standout in the entire Star Trek franchise.

This week, Bill and Dan take on this two-part epic discussing what makes this episode so different and how, for one of them, this really was a defining moment for Star Trek: Voyager.

item.title: 2020 Outtakes Special, Part 1
item.description:
2020 is coming to a close and that means it's time for a long standing tradition here on Trek Geeks!

In 2015, we started to include our pre/post-show banter as a little clip after the end theme of the podcast and, since then, it's really taken on a life of its own. It provides a tiny glimpse into our friendship and typically has us laughing quite a bit.

This week and next week we have for you our two part 2020 Outtakes Special, featuring all of the "Bing Bong" moments from throughout the year.

We hope you all have a safe, happy, and healthy New Year!
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: SyFy Sistas Talk - Star Trek Picard (Episode 7, Nepenthe)
item.description:
In our third podcast Tamia, Fran T, and I delve into #107 which is a well-deserved break in the action of Picard's journey. Nepenthe is an episode full of rich ST TNG memories and new observations that bring surprising facets out of each of us.

Correction: The Story of Helen of Troy (yes, we went there) is from the Iliad, not the Odyssey. It was a slip of the tongue y'all, please don't call me. LOL

Thanks to our terrific sound engineer DoS The Anonymous 1 Production. You can reach him for YOUR podcast at dostheanonymous1@gmail.com

Find the SyFy Sistas online www.syfysistas.com

Facebook @SyFy Sistas

Twitter @SyFy Sistas

Instagram syfy.sistas
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: SPECIAL PREVIEW: The Divine Treasury
item.description:
TREK GEEKS LISTENERS:

We hope you'll enjoy this special, full-length preview of the first episode of a new podcast on the Trek Geeks Network!

The Divine Treasury: A Star Trek Collectibles Podcast

"Connecting Through Collecting."

The Divine Treasury delves into collecting all things Star Trek. This podcast will feature conversations with collectors as they talk about the wonderful Star Trek treasures that they collect, share, and sell. More importantly, this podcast will examine the stories behind their collections, what drove them to collect, and which items hold the most important places in their hearts. Other episodes will include topics about the science of collecting and will feature interviews with industry professionals, such as Star Trek Licensees, manufacturers, suppliers, authenticators, and graders.

The Divine Treasury is available now wherever podcasts are found and also on TrekGeeks.com!
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks: A Star Trek Podcast

item.description:
2020 is coming to a close and that means it's time for a long standing tradition here on Trek Geeks!
