item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: Farking Awesome, Volume 1
item.description:
In the second year of the Trek Geeks Podcast, Dan started doing puns during our weekly thanks to the band Five Year Mission. Before too long, those evolved into Farkisms--where he'd take the name of drummer Andy Fark and turn that into some kind of pun on an episode name.

For years, people have been asking us of an episode of just the Farkisms, and there are so many, we had to break it up! So, this week, enjoy our first installment of Farkisms in "Farking Awesome, Volume 1!"
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: SyFy Sistas Talk - Star Trek Picard (Episode 8, 9, and 10) The Resolution
item.description:
SyFy Sistas Tamia, FranT, and Subrina complete their deep dive of Season 1 of Star Trek: Picard with this three episode review. There is a mini art history lesson, special shout outs to the second unit production crew, FranT's love of "Miss" Ryan, and Tamia's onpoint head scratchers. But, will the Sistas be satisfied with the telling of end of dear, dear friend?

You can find the SyFy Sistas at https://syfysistas.com

Facebook www.facebook.com/syfysistas

They are also on Twitter and Instagram as syfysistas

The producers of SyFy Sistas podcast want to give a special shout out to their sound engineer who also provides all the original music heard on these episodes, DoS, the Anonymous One. You can reach him to help you with YOUR podcast at dostheanonymous1@gmail.com
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: A Conversation with Noah Averbach-Katz
item.description:
In this special crossover episode of Discovering Trek, Dan and Bill are honored to have Noah Averbach-Katz join them for a special discussion about his time on Star Trek: Discovery Season 3 as the Andorian, Ryn. The character quickly became one of the most popular of the season and Noah got to live out every Star Trek fans dream by playing a role in our beloved universe.

During our conversation, we discuss his fandom when he was young, what it was like to be directed by the Star Trek legend Jonathan Frakes, and what it was like to work with his wife Mary Wiseman, aka Sylvia Tilly. Along the way we also find out just how...interesting it was to film scenes with the Queen herself - Grudge the cat!

Bill and Dan cannot thank Noah enough for joining them on the podcast, and they hope you enjoy the conversation as much as they did on Discovering Trek!

FIVE YEAR MISSION

Music for Discovering Trek is provided by Five Year Mission. They're writing one song for each episode of The Original Series! Plus, check out their podcast right here on the Trek Geeks Podcast Network!

Find out more and download all 6 of their current albums at FiveYearMission.net
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks: A Star Trek Podcast

item.title: Goodbye 2020, Hello 2021
item.description:
This week, we're back with our first new episode of 2021! We'll take a look at the big events in Trek from 2020 and look ahead to the coming year to see what's in store.

Joining us is TrekNews.net contributor Kyle Hadyniak to look at some of our favorite news form last year.

Plus, the 6th Anniversary of Trek Geeks and news for the month of February!

FIVE YEAR MISSION

The music of Trek Geeks comes from none other than Five Year Mission and Episode 25 of Five Year Mission: The Podcast is available now on the Trek Geeks Podcast Network!

From concept to characters and beyond, they'll talk about why they love this series and why the appreciation for it has done nothing but grow over the years.

Plus, we'll talk about some of our personal favorite episodes from Enterprise, as well as some of yours!

item.description:
IT'S TIME FOR THE NEWS FROM TREKNEWS.NET!

This week, Bill & Dan discuss the new audiobook narrator for "The Autobiography of Kathryn Janeway, the upcoming Star Trek: Voyager documentary from 455 Films, and a new look for CBS All Access.
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: Nichelle & Uhura (w/SyFy Sistas)
item.description:
This week on Trek Geeks, we begin a month-long series of special episodes for Black History Month.

We wanted to elevate the voices in our fandom that we thought you needed to hear so all February long, we'll be featuring content from African American podcasters from our Star Trek fandom! Bill and Dan will bookend each episode, but we hope you enjoy the special content that has been put together by these amazingly talented and wonderful people.

Up first, our friends the SyFy Sistas will talk about the legacies of Nichelle Nichols and her iconic character Lt. Nyota Uhura, as well as what both of these women mean to them personally.

SYFY SISTAS PODCAST

We are so truly grateful to Tamia, Fran, Subrina, Yvette, and JD for sharing their creativity, their positivity and their Star Trek fandom with the Trek Geeks audience.

You can find out more about the SyFy Sistas on their website, syfysistas.com, and their fantastic podcast can be found just about everywhere.

Plus, you can find them on Twitter, Facebook, and Instagram so be sure to give them a follow!
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: Black History Month Tribute to the Queen of Star Trek Ms Nichelle Nichols
item.description:
The SyFy Sistas kick off Black History Month, 2021, as guest hosts on the Trek Geeks Podcast Network. The topic for this honor is, of course, a session discussing the sista who start it all, Lt. Uhura, played by our Queen, Ms Nichelle Nichols. Join Fran, J.D., Subrina, Tamia, and Yvette as they recount their first impressions on this true icon, what she means to them, and that day at Shore Leave in Maryland when they actually met her in person.

Thank you Dan and Bill of Trek Geek for sharing your platform. And, thanks to our sound guru, DoS, the Anonymous 1.

You can reach DoS at dostheanonymous1@gmail.com

You can reach the Sistas at our website SyFy Sistas!

On Facebook @Syfysistas

Twitter: SyFy Sistas

IG: syfy.sistas
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.description:
IT'S TIME FOR THE NEWS FROM TREKNEWS.NET!

This week, Bill & Dan discuss a new documentary featuring the iconic Nichelle Nichols, a new mem-noir from a Next Gen favorite, and cryptic comments from Alex Kurtzman!

PLUS--A CONTEST!

FanSets is giving away a set of brand new pins that won't be available to the general public for some time, and you have the chance to win them before ANYBODY else! Listen to this week's episode to find out how!

item.title: Jake Sisko (with David Majors)
item.description:
This week on Trek Geeks, we continue our month-long series of special episodes for Black History Month.

We wanted to elevate the voices in our fandom that we thought you needed to hear so all February long, we'll be featuring content from African American podcasters from our Star Trek fandom! Bill and Dan will bookend each episode, but we hope you enjoy the special content that has been put together by these amazingly talented and wonderful people.

In this episode, our friend David Majors from the Promenade Merchants Podcast will talk about an often over-looked character in the Star Trek Universe: Deep Space Nine's Jake Sisko! Joining him in the discussion to look at this pivotal character is his sister Maimunah!

PROMENADE MERCHANTS PODCAST Our sincere thanks go out to David and Maimunah for sharing their thoughts and their voices with the Trek Geeks audience!

You can find out more about the Promenade Merchants Podcast which features David and co-host Heather Kirby on their Twitter page: @PromTrekPod and download their podcast on your favorite podcast app.

item.description:
IT'S TIME FOR THE NEWS FROM TREKNEWS.NET!

This week, Bill & Dan discuss an award nomination for Hanelle Culpepper, digital distribution for the Nichelle Nichols documentary, "Woman in Motion," and the passing of a Hollywood legend.

PLUS--A CONTEST!

FanSets is giving away a set of brand new pins that won't be available to the general public for some time, and you have the chance to win them before ANYBODY else! Listen to this week's episode to find out how!

item.title: Celebrating of Black Love on Deep Space Nine with Special Guest Cirroc (Jake Sisko) Lofton
item.description:
We said we had a BIG surprise and it is epic. The SyFy Sistas welcome our very first guest to the podcast, none other than Mr. Cirroc Lofton. Cirroc settles in to discuss Black Love on DS9 in all it's iterations. But, we also cover his experiences growing up as a working actor, his special relationship with Avery (Capt. Ben Sisko) Brooks, and the TV landscape in the 90s. Grab a snack, settle in, and enjoy a Valentine's Day show with Cirroc and the Sistas.

You can find the store Cirroc mentions at Original Designs, Ethiopian Crosses & Ethiopian Goods – Abyssinian Kiosk

Cirroc's podcast: (7) The 7th Rule - YouTube

As always special thanks to our sound engineer, DoS, the Anonymous. You can reach him at dostheanonymous1@gmail.com

You can reach us at SyFy Sistas! and shop for stuff at SyFy Sistas (teespring.com)
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: Far Beyond the Stars (w/Lance Laster & Kevonte Chilous)
item.description:
This week on Trek Geeks, we continue our month-long series of special episodes for Black History Month.

We wanted to elevate the voices in our fandom that we thought you needed to hear so all February long, we'll be featuring content from African American podcasters from our Star Trek fandom! Bill and Dan will bookend each episode, but we hope you enjoy the special content that has been put together by these amazingly talented and wonderful people.

In this episode, we welcome Lance Laster & Kevonte Chilous from "Always Hold On to Arrow" on the United Federation of Podcasts to discuss the truly legendary Deep Space Nine episode, Far Beyond the Stars. No matter how many times you've seen this episode, this conversation will have you look at it differently on your next watch.

ALWAYS HOLD ON TO ARROW

You can find out more about Lance & Kevonte's podcast, Always Hold On to Arrow, at the United Federation of Podcasts or by searching on your favorite podcast app.
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.description:
IT'S TIME FOR THE NEWS FROM TREKNEWS.NET!

This week, Bill & Dan discuss a new Star Trek game for your browser where you could win some big prizes, Ethan Peck's Instagram post from Toronto, and a return of The Outrageous Okona?

We'll catch you up on the biggest stories of the week in less than 15 minutes on The Oldest Brand-New Star Trek News Podcast: The News From TrekNews.net!

YOU KNOW WE'RE GONNA SAY IT...

For all the newz on all the Star Trekz, yo...please visit our good friends at TrekNews.net, online at treknews.net!
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: 007- Star Trek Captains and their First Officers
item.description:
Starship captains are a man or woman in a million, and their first officers are in unique professional and personal relationships with them. What makes these crucial partnerships work or fail to work? How do age, gender, and even species affect the chain of command? Listen as SyFy Sistas Tamia, Fran, and Subrina break down your favorite duos: Kirk and Spock, Picard and Riker, Sisko and Kira, Janeway and Chakotay, Archer and T'Pol, and Georgiou and Burnham, from their point of view.

Reach us at our website: syfysistas.com Emails: subrina@syfysistas.com, tamia@syfysistas.com, and frant@syfysistas.com Twitter: @SyfySistas IG: syfy.sistas FB: SyFy Sistas

Special thanks to our sound engineer DoS, the Anonymous One Reach DoS at dostheanonymous1@gmail.com
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.description:
IT'S TIME FOR THE NEWS FROM TREKNEWS.NET!

This week, a big opening day for the Voyager Documentary on Indiegogo, a first look at Star Trek: Prodigy characters, more tiny starships, and we have a winner of the FanSets Pinmoji characters!

item.description:
This week, Bill & Dan reconvene to discuss the state of the Star Trek franchise, Trek conventions, and Trek's very fandom itself!

What do they think is going right--and what, in particular, do they think is not going well at all? Find out in this week's episode of The Flagship.

We'll be joined by our friend and PoliTreks co-host Barry DeFord to discuss the themes for our episode marathons and which installments we've selected to watch!

item.description:
IT'S TIME FOR THE NEWS FROM TREKNEWS.NET!

This week: new cast members for Star Trek: Strange New Worlds, a new writer for yet another attempt at a Trek film, new heights for the Voyager Documentary crowdfunding, and a new deal for the Hageman brothers!
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: 008 - It's Possible: From Brandy to Burnham
item.description:
The Sistas discuss the impact of the 1997 TV movie Cinderella, starring Brandy Norwood and Whitney Houston, on multiple generations of Black women. The beauty of diversity is the main theme of this memorable TV event. Did the Whitney Houston production of this classic fairy tale with its color-blind cast help lay the foundation for Black leading ladies to come? We draw a line from Brandy's Princess Throne to Burnham's Captain's chair and ask, "Do science fiction and fantasy productions of today do 'color-blind' as well as this 1997 gem?

You can find the SyFy Sistas online at syfysistas.com Facebook @SyFy Sistas Twitter: @Syfy Sistas Instagram: @syfy.sistas

And we give a special shout out to our sound engineer DoS, the Anonymous. Find him at: dostheanonymous1@gmail.com
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: The News From TrekNews.Net: 03/25/2021
item.description:
IT'S TIME FOR THE NEWS FROM TREKNEWS.NET!

This week, Bill & Dan discuss a milestone birthday for The Captain, an award for Hanelle Culpepper, a special day for Leonard Nimoy in The Hub of the Universe, and an update on the Voyager Documentary--including a new name!
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: Acquisition
item.description:
As we continue our celebration of #Enterprise20, we return after our impromptu week off with an episode that Dan selected...

...so blame him.

No, really.

Season 1's "Acquisition" is an episode that divides fandom: It's either great fan service or it's terrible fan service. This week, you'll get to decide! We'll tackle this MEHpisode and look at the things that worked, as well as a lot of things that didn't.
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: Lido Deck's 200th Episode
item.description:
After five years, we bring you the landmark 200th episode of Lido Deck: A Love Boat Podcast!

Kicking off Season 6: Gopher is kidnapped by a lookalike, a young woman resists an arranged marriage, and divorce looms on a couple's 50th anniversary.

Join us in Italy for Lido Deck: Episode 200!

Special thanks to the Rumpus Room Podcast Network, the home of Lido Deck and other great classic television podcasts!
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks: A Star Trek Podcast

item.title: The News From TrekNews.net: 04/01/2021
item.description:
IT'S TIME FOR THE NEWS FROM TREKNEWS.NET!

This week, Bill and Dan discuss a memorial for Leonard Nimoy in his hometown, First Contact Day events, new Star Trek merch, and The Biggest Thing of the Week: the Voyager Documentary finishes its crowdfunding!

tact Day events, new Star Trek merch, and The Biggest Thing of the Week: the Voyager Documentary finishes its crowdfunding!
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: 009 - It's STNG "Darmok" vs Doctor Who's "Midnight"
item.description:
SyFy Sistas J.D., Tamia, and Subrina visit that other celebrated sci-fi franchise, Doctor Who.

It's STNG's fan favorite "Darmok" and the episode of Doctor Who "Midnight". Both episodes examine the frustration and fear induced by the inability to communicate. But the REAL drama is will J.D.'s diabolical episode pairing turn Tamia and Subrina into Tardis loving Whovians?

You can find the SyFy Sistas online at syfysistas.com Facebook @SyFy Sistas Twitter: @Syfy Sistas Instagram: @syfy.sistas

And we give a special shout out to our sound engineer DoS, the Anonymous One. Find him at dostheanonymous1@gmail.com
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: First Contact Day
item.description:
This past week, Star Trek fans celebrated First Contact Day and Star Trek did not disappoint. We were treated to great panels, surprise teaser trailers, and a big announcement and, this week, we'll talk about it all!

Joining us to break down all the day's events is TrekNews.net contributor, Kyle Hadyniak. How did we feel about the new Discovery teaser, Hologram Janeway, and Chicago? Find out this week!

FIVE YEAR MISSION

The music of Trek Geeks comes from none other than Five Year Mission and Episode 34 of Five Year Mission: The Podcast is available now on the Trek Geeks Podcast Network! This time, the guys have another installment of "What Are Little Songs Made Of?" as they look the episode "A Taste of Armageddon" and the song it inspired!

Subscribe now on Apple Podcasts, Google Podcasts, Spotify, and wherever you download podcasts!

5YM, of course, is every note of music you hear on Rewind, and we couldn't be more grateful. Go get yourself a copy of all their CDs: Year 1, Year 2, Year 3, Year 4, Spock's Brain, and The Trouble With Tribbles. It's easy head on over to FiveYearMission.net RIGHT NOW and get that physical media in your hands! We just KNOW you'll become huge fans of the band and their music!
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: Jonathan Archer
item.description:
He's supposed to be the role model from which all other Captains gain inspiration, but is he?

This week, Bill is joined by Discovering Trek's Kasey Shafsky (sitting in for Dan) to discuss this prototype captain and the qualities that make him who he is, and why he was perfect for the job he had.
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: The News From TrekNews.net: 04/15/2021
item.description:
IT'S TIME FOR THE NEWS FROM TREKNEWS.NET!

This week, a new Star Trek mobile game, a new date for a new Star Trek film, and a huge award for Star Trek: Discovery!

Join Bill & Dan for The News From TrekNews.net--the Oldest Brand-New Star Trek News Podcast from Trek Geeks!
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: 010 - The Sisters Who Save The S*#t…um, SHIP.
item.description:
Latina/Klingon B'Elanna Torres of Star Trek: Voyager and Belter Naomi Nagata of The Expanse, are our favorite chief engineers. Besides being two of the smartest characters in sci-fi, they also have enthralling story arcs that border on 'messy'. All flux couplers being equal, our final question is, "Who would you want on your side in a fire fight?"

You can find the SyFy Sistas online at syfysistas.com Facebook @SyFy Sistas Twitter: @Syfy Sistas Instagram: @syfy.sistas

And we give a special shout out to our sound engineer DoS, the Anonymous One. Find him at dostheanonymous1@gmail.com
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: The News From TrekNews.net: 04/22/2021
item.description:
IT'S TIME FOR THE NEWS FROM TREKNEWS.NET!

This week, Bill & Dan discuss new high-end action figures, Star Trek army men, and a giant tease on social media from a TNG alum...that might have backfired?
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: 011 - Michael Burnham Kirkin' It Up?
item.description:
Black female lead on the bridge is an idea that sent a vocal minority of Star Trek fans into a feedback loop. But relax, because Michael Burnham is really our hero, James T. Kirk! Listen as the Sistas demonstrate how Burnham and Kirk, captains from the same era, are far more alike than you think.

You can find the SyFy Sistas online at syfysistas.com Facebook @SyFy Sistas Twitter: @Syfy Sistas Instagram: @syfy.sistas

And we give a special shout out to our sound engineer DoS, the Anonymous One. Find him at dostheanonymous1@gmail.com
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: Resolutions
item.description:
Voyager's Captain and First Officer have contracted a virus from an insect bite on an alien world and they've been marooned on the surface to live out the rest of their lives...but what will this do to their relationship and what will it mean for the future?

...I think we all know the answer to that one. ;)

This week, Bill and Kasey take on the penultimate episode of Voyager's second season, "Resolutions." (Or, "The One Where Janeway & Chakotay Might Do It. Or Not. Probably Not.)
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: Gates McFadden
item.description:
This week, we not only see the return of Dan Davidson, but we also have an incredibly special guest! We welcome none other than Star Trek icon Gates McFadden to the Trek Geeks Podcast!

We'll talk about her brand new podcast where she talks to a lot of her Star Trek family, "Gates McFadden InvestiGates: Who Do You Think You Are?" and what it's like to go from zero to podcasting in the course of a pandemic.

Gates' new podcast is available on all podcasting platforms, so be sure to check it out today! Episode 1 is a conversation with her good friend, Jonathan Frakes!
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: 012 - The Sam & Bucky Show
item.description:
From a Sista in the Seat to a Brother with the Shield, J.D. leads Fran, Subrina, Tamia, and Yvette into a deep discussion that covers DC versus Marvel comics, code switching, big sisters and little brothers, and all sorts of Marvel Easter eggs. Yes, we are back in series recap mode, and no, it isn't Star Trek, but count on the SyFy Sistas to get some Trek references into this conversation.

You can find the SyFy Sistas online at syfysistas.com Facebook @SyFy Sistas Twitter: @Syfy Sistas Instagram: @syfy.sistas

And we give a special shout out to our sound engineer DoS, the Anonymous One. Find him at dostheanonymous1@gmail.com
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: When You Wish Upon a Trek
item.description:
This week, Bill & Dan spend a lot of time wishing and hoping! They've come up with their ultimate wish lists of actors they'd love to see in Star Trek someday as well as some characters they might play. We think some of the picks might surprise you! Of course, there are rules:

  • 6-10 actors, none of whom can be Lance Reddick
  • Actors must be living at time of recording
  • Can't ever have been in Star Trek in any capacity (no guest stars, no extras)
  • Someone you'd want to see as a series regular, either in a current series or a theoretical, made-up future series or reboot.
  • Be specific as to the role you'd like to see them play and why.

Who is on YOUR ultimate wish list? Let us know!
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: 013 - Recap of The Sam and Bucky Show - Episode 2
item.description:
The Sistas react viscerally to the happenings in the Falcon and the Winter Soldier #102. While Tamia does her best to keep the discussion on track and J.D. drops more incredible Easter Eggs, Yvette takes us to the Lord of the Rings, Subrina makes a temporal incursion to Episode 3, Fran is clear about where she stands on John Walker, and we ALL have something to say about Lemar. From competitive therapy to secret experiments on Black G.I.s this episode from Marvel Studios churns up some serious stuff...and we loved it. Plus, try to guess which one of the Sistas brings the whole discourse back to Star Trek this week.

You can find the SyFy Sistas online at syfysistas.com Facebook @SyFy Sistas Twitter: @Syfy Sistas Instagram: @syfy.sistas

And we give a special shout out to our sound engineer DoS, the Anonymous One. Find him at dostheanonymous1@gmail.com
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: Nepenthe
item.description:
This week, Dan and Bill return to take their first-ever look at an episode from Star Trek: Picard--and what better episode to do that with than the fan-favorite, "Nepenthe!"

The seventh episode of Picard's first season, Jean-Luc Picard and Soji seek shelter from Will Riker and Deanna Troi, while Hugh assists Elnor on the Artifact and Agnes Jurati looks for a way out. There's a lot that happens in this one and even more emotion.

We'll look at all the developments that happen in Nepenthe and why we think this episode is so amazing.
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: 014 - Recap of The Sam and Bucky Show - Episode 3
item.description:
Things get real in "Power Broker" as Sam and Bucky are involved in a jailbreak and spy antics in the search for Karli, the Angry Child. The Sistas discuss poor Bucky's reprise as the Winter Soldier. Yvette gets him, Subrina thinks he's alright, Tamia thinks he's layered, but J.D. nails his problem to the wall with millennial accuracy. While Tamia tackles Zemo and iconography, and Yvette explains Walker and Lemar, we learn what J.D. thinks about all three of them. Subrina goes deep into "Trouble Man", while Fran drops love for...swing music. Evil Madripoor, wicked sartorial choices, and a killer cameo round out FAWS Episode 3. But can the Sistas continue their Star Trek reference streak? Who's quoting Dr. Michio Kaku and why? And how did Fiats get into this?

You can find the SyFy Sistas online at syfysistas.com Facebook @SyFy Sistas Twitter: @Syfy Sistas Instagram: @syfy.sistas

And we give a special shout out to our sound engineer DoS, the Anonymous One. Find him at dostheanonymous1@gmail.com
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: The News From TrekNews.net: 06/17/2021
item.description:
This week, Bill & Dan look at a lot of news from a lot of Star Trek shows in production, new Mirror Universe comics, and the cast announcement for Star Trek: Prodigy!

Plus, get a bonus segment on the Star Trek: Picard teaser trailer only available on the Trek Geeks mobile app!

iOS/Apple Appstore: https://apps.apple.com/us/app/trek-geeks/id1569873516

Google Play: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.podsquad.trekgeekspodcast
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: Vegas Convention Preview with Adam Malin
item.description:
It's been a long time since we've been able with our Trek Family in Las Vegas, and this summer we're finally back in the desert to celebrate this franchise we all love.

Joining us to talk about the 50th Anniversary of Creation Entertainment and to preview some of what you'll see during the show is Creation's Co-CEO, Adam Malin!

You've seen him on stage asking questions of our Star Trek stars, and now we'll ask him all about getting the first Star Trek convention off the ground and the challenges in scheduling, rescheduling, and rescheduling the 2021 show!

Plus, Adam makes a special reveal about the 2022 show that you're not gonna want to miss!

Don't forget to go to creationent.com to get all the info to prepare for this year's convention and we'll see you in Vegas!
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: 015 - Recap of The Sam & Bucky Show: - Episode 4
item.description:
J.D. continues to take the point on our FAWS deep dive where the Sistas ask: Can you be a superhero without being super? Yvette keeps it real deconstructing the dilemma of lost people. We get back to the everyday with a Tina Fey reference, a discussion of snatching Lemar's Black Card, and Tom Hanks in Castaway. While Tamia wants more on the Blip, J.D. yes, J.D. sees the human side of John Walker. And just wait until we get to (spoiler alert) the entrance of the Dora Milaje.

You can find the SyFy Sistas online at syfysistas.com

  • Facebook @SyFy Sistas
  • Twitter: @Syfy Sistas
  • Instagram: @syfy.sistas

And we give a special shout out to our sound engineer DoS, the Anonymous One. Find him at dostheanonymous1@gmail.com
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: The Andorians
item.description:
This week, we welcome Star Trek: Discovery's Ryn, Noah Averbach-Katz, to discuss an race in Star Trek he might know a little about.

We'll discuss everybody's favorite ice-planet dwelling xenophobes, the Andorians! We'll get some special insight from Noah on bringing Ryn to life as well as some of the complexities of this alien's dealings.

Plus, we bring you another in our series of "How to Vegas," with our good friend Ron Wrobel! This week, it's all about the budget and we'll give you some ideas on how you can maximize yours when you're there for the con!
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: See It or Skip It: Enterprise S1
item.description:
FINALLY

It's been a long road...getting to this See It or Skip It, but here we are and, this week, we're taking a look at Season 1 of Star Trek: Enterprise! We're looking at all 26 episodes and, well, you know how it goes: some of them we'll see and some of them we won't even remember.

Joining us as special Guest Referee is Sara from Rewind and Discovering Trek: Enterprise! She'll try to keep us on track as we talk about each episode which, as you know, is no small task...but we have faith...of the...well, you know.
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: 016 - Recap of The Sam and Bucky Show - Episode 5
item.description:
This episode is emotional for the SyFy Sistas as Yvette breaks down the role of a good soldier being a "good soldier," Tamia breaks down 'erasure' and Isaiah Bradley near breaks down the Sistas. But they bounce right back with an impromptu Black History lesson (see list below).

Leave it to J.D. to let us know that 'not my monkey, not my circus' is a thing and give us the Marvel dossier on the Contessa. There are shout outs to the deft acting of Daniel Bruhl as Zemo, Gabrielle Byndloss as Olivia Walker, and Florence Kasumba as the Dora Milaje leader, Ayo. Tamia and J.D. are so done with shield practice montages, and you'll never guess who makes the first Star Trek reference.

The following documentaries were mentioned: High on the Hog: How African American Cuisine Transformed America - Netflix Africa's Great Civilizations, hosted by Prof. Henry Louis Gates – Prime Video The 1619 Project Docuseries – Hulu

You can find the SyFy Sistas online at syfysistas.com Facebook @SyFy Sistas Twitter: @Syfy Sistas Instagram: @syfy.sistas

And we give a special shout out to our sound engineer DoS, the Anonymous One. Find him at dostheanonymous1@gmail.com
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: Rod Roddenberry
item.description:
This week, Bill and Dan are joined by none other than Star Trek: Discovery/Picard/Lower Decks/Prodigy/Strange New Worlds executive producer, Rod Roddenberry to talk about the 100th birthday of his father, Gene Roddenberry!

We'll discuss the legacy of Gene as his centennial approaches, as well as "Gene's Vision," and Rod's thoughts fandom, storytelling in Star Trek, what he's learned in the ten years since his documentary "Trek Nation" was released, as well as the Roddenberry #thinkTREK campaign!

Plus, we'll have another How to Vegas segment with our good friend, Ron Wrobel in this episode of The Flagship!
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: Meteorologist Katie Teaches Weather to Two Idiots
item.description:
What happens when a Force of Nature tries to teach weather to two Natural Disasters? Well, wonder no more!

This week, Bill and Dan are joined by Meteorologist Katie Nickolaou from CBS 14 in Sioux City, Iowa to talk about her fandom, the extreme weather we've been experiencing across the country, Star Trek, and...PLINKO?! Yep. That's right. Plinko!

Plus, more on "How to Vegas," with Mr. Convention himself--Ron Wrobel!

Less than three weeks to go until the 55 Year Mission in Vegas, so download this episode and get ready!
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: The News From TrekNews.net: 07/22/2021
item.description:
This week, Bill and Dan cover the latest Star Trek movie news, new Star Trek action figures and playsets coming our way, and Emmy nominations!

Plus, get a bonus segment on the recently announced tickets for Star Trek: Mission Chicago only available on the Trek Geeks mobile app!

iOS/Apple Appstore: https://apps.apple.com/us/app/trek-geeks/id1569873516

Google Play: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.podsquad.trekgeekspodcast
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: 017 - Recap of The Sam and Bucky Show Finale - "One World, One People"
item.description:
We come to the end of the Falcon's story. And, while the Sistas are okay with "a Black man carrying the Stars and Stripes" they are split on the new costume. Yvette and Tamia won't give Bucky a break about that phone call, and everybody is so done, done, done with Karli, that J.D. must explain Marvel's lack of villain back stories. And despite the plunging helicopters, falling armored cars, and well, fighting, fighting, and more fighting, Subrina finds her first Marvel Easter Egg. Yes, we can say that J.D. has completed her mission – the SyFy Sistas are now good with the MCU. Bring on the Multiverse!

You can find the SyFy Sistas online at syfysistas.com - Facebook @SyFy Sistas - Twitter: @Syfy Sistas - Instagram: @syfy.sistas And: trekgeeks.com/syfysistas

We give a special shout out to our sound engineer DoS, the Anonymous. Find him at dostheanonymous1@gmail.com
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: Anthony Rapp
item.description:
With just two weeks to go until Vegas, we're chatting briefly with Star Trek: Discovery's Anthony Rapp about Season 3 now that you can get it from your favorite retailer on DVD, Blu-Ray, and Steelbook! We'll talk about the importance of Discovery jumping to the year 32nd century, some of the challenges, and more!

Plus, Bill and Dan talk favorite Season 3 moments and another of our "How to Vegas" segments with "Mr. Convention," Ron Wrobel!
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: 018 - The LeVar Burton Love Jam
item.description:
As LeVar Burton wraps up his week guest hosting Jeopardy, the SyFy Sistas celebrate him with a look back at Star Trek: TNG and its Chief Engineer, Geordi LaForge. But being the Sistas, they also discuss a serious omission in Picard Season 1, an 'if-only' alternate ending to the Leah Brahms storyline, a theory about why the Romulans mess with Geordi, and what was the deal all the attitude in Relics?

We give a special shout out to our sound engineer DoS, the Anonymous. Find him at dostheanonymous1@gmail.com

You can find the SyFy Sistas online at syfysistas.com - Facebook @SyFy Sistas - Twitter: @Syfy Sistas - Instagram: @syfy.sistas - And of course, trekgeeks.com/syfysistas
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: Best Laid Plans
item.description:
To say we've been getting excited for Las Vegas and the 55 Year Mission Tour convention would be a vast understatement. As recently as a few days ago, we were preparing to make our trip to the desert.

...and then we changed our plans.

This week, we pre-empt our planned episode on The Royale to discuss the decision to cancel FanGeeks Party 2021 and our trip to Las Vegas in the wake of the Delta Variant.

Plus, we'll be joined by Lew Halboth from FanSets to discuss why they are still going to Vegas and what you can expect to see from them for new releases! Oh, and listen for an impromptu giveaway of a huge assortment of FanSets pins, too!

And if you thought we wouldn't have the last of our "How to Vegas" series with Ron Wrobel, you'd be wrong! We'll talk about what to do once you get there--just keep in mind it was recorded before our decision.

We hope everyone travelling to Vegas stays safe and healthy. Enjoy yourselves and look out for one another, because we'll sure miss you!
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: 019 - B'Elanna: Before and After
item.description:
This week the SyFy Sistas sit down with Lucila M or Dr. Luz to talk about our other favorite Chief Engineer, B'Elanna Torres. She is the sister who saves the ship; brilliant, passionate, and loyal. But she is also a sister with deep-seated issues. Our talk with Dr. Luz ranges from B'Elanna 'sciencing' her way out of trouble, making Tom Paris grow up, and facing her inner demons. Do we mention your favorite B'Elanna moment? Tune in and find out.

We give a special shout out to our sound engineer DoS, the Anonymous. Find him at dostheanonymous1@gmail.com

You can find the SyFy Sistas online at syfysistas.com Facebook @SyFy Sistas Twitter: @Syfy Sistas Instagram: @syfy.sistas And of course, www.trekgeeks.com/syfysistas
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: THE BEST OF TREK GEEKS: Nana Visitor
item.description:
Originally Published: April 10, 2015

This week, Bill is having his wisdom teeth out so, in lieu of a brand new episode, we're sharing one of our favorite early conversations on Trek Geeks!

In 2015, we were insanely lucky to have Nana Visitor from Star Trek: Deep Space Nine on the podcast to discuss creating the Kira character, women and the Star Trek Universe, how DS9 has held up over time, how a new generation of fans is discovering the series, and even a little Friday the 13th talk!

We hope you'll enjoy this Best of Trek Geeks episode--or enjoy it again! We'll be back with new episodes soon!
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: 020 - Malissa Longo of the 7th Rule Podcast
item.description:
Join us for a long-awaited chat with the beautiful, talented, and resilient Malissa Longo, co-host of the 7th Rule Podcast with Cirroc Lofton and Ryan Husk. Malissa is also a business owner and the wife of our beloved Aron Eisenberg. But Malissa is a true multi-hyphenate, dancer, actor, and artist in her own right.

The conversation runs from Subrina's beleaguered defense of musicals to the uniqueness of the Star Trek community, Nana Visitor love, Malissa's new acting gig that may go to TV pilot to who is dodging Armin's Shakespeare course. J.D. brings us back from a Q rabbit hole with her love of Malissa's cuff bracelets and wearable art. And finally, Yvette, Tamia, and Fran pose questions we can all get behind, one of which is "Are there any Black Orions?"

- Malissa Longo's store: www.walkingartmadebymalissa.com - The 7th Rule Podcast: The 7th Rule - YouTube - Chase Masterson's Foundation: www.BeKindMerch.org - Armin Shimerman's Online Course: https://antaeus.org/academy_classes/mastering-shakespeare/

We give a special shout out to our sound engineer DoS, the Anonymous. Find him at dostheanonymous1@gmail.com You can find the SyFy Sistas online at syfysistas.com - Facebook @SyFy Sistas - Twitter: @Syfy Sistas - Instagram: @syfy.sistas - And of course, www.trekgeeks.com/syfysistas
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: Star Trek Day
item.description:
This week, The Flagship is back! Bill and Dan return after a somewhat unplanned absence to talk about the developments from Star Trek Day!

There were lots of announcements, some trailers, and some premiere dates. We'll break it all down and explain why Star Trek Day is now one of our favorite days of the year!
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: 021 - Picard and the Sistas: Guinan, Lily, and Raffi
item.description:
The SyFy Sistas look at the special relationship Captain Jean-Luc Picard maintains with three amazing Black women; Guinan, the El-Aurian bartender from his days on the Enterprise-D, Lily Sloane from his temporal incursion in the film Star Trek: First Contact (1996), and Raffaela (Raffi) Musiker, XO during his command of the Romulan evacuation and later his quest companion in the series Picard.

These three women have the ear of the Captain of the Enterprise D and E, the flagship of the Federation fleet, the Chief Contact with the Q Continuum, the Arbiter of Succession for the Klingon Empire, the Savior of Earth from the Borg Invasion, the face of the United Federation of Planets, and a few other things that only Yvette calls him. Our discussion ranges from their conversational styles to our appreciation of the TWA over ugly-ass wigs. But wait until you hear what Fran saw coming that no other SyFy Sista did.

We give a special shout out to our sound engineer DoS, the Anonymous. Find him at dostheanonymous1@gmail.com You can find the SyFy Sistas online at syfysistas.com

Facebook @SyFy Sistas Twitter: @SyfySistas Instagram: @syfy.sistas And of course, www.trekgeeks.com/syfysistas
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: All Our Yesterdays
item.description:
The Enterprise arrives at the planet Sarpeidon which is about to be destroyed by a supernova. Kirk, Spock, and McCoy beam down to investigate and are trapped in a planet's distant past--where Kirk is accused of being a witch and Spock finds love (or something) with an exiled woman.

We'll discuss why we love this episode despite its obvious quirks and plot holes. Where does Spock think he's going to get seeds? Why doesn't McCoy regress? And what the hell happens to that cart once it goes through the Atavachron portal, anyway?

Join Bill & Dan for All Our Yesterdays, this week on The Flagship!
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: John Billingsley
item.description:
Parents: This episode is tagged as explicit for adult language and situations. There is no "bleeping" in this episode.

This week, we're excited to welcome John Billingsley to The Flagship!

For four seasons, he portrayed the NX-01's Chief Medical Officer, Dr. Phlox and today he stops by to talk a little bit about the 20th Anniversary of Star Trek: Enterprise.

In addition, we'll talk to him about an organization he's passionate about: the Hollywood Food Coalition! They've been feeding the homeless in the Los Angeles area for nearly 35 years and the organization has never missed a night.

Plus, Dan throws out an idea that is pretty awesome--and something you'll be hearing more about very soon!
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: 022 - Collecting 101 with The Divine Treasury
item.description:
It's our first Trek Geeks Crossover Show with the Divine Treasury. The fun we have with our Fandom Friday social media posts on the Muthaship group page made the Sistas curious about collecting. So, we decided to go to the pros, Mike Bovia and Jamie Rogers, host of our sister show The Divine Treasury, for an overview on collecting. Jamie gives us critical intel on how to start (go with your passion), where to go for valuations (eBay is your friend), and how to take care of your hoard (don't skimp). From cards, to films, to action figures, to comic books, we run through it all.

What the Sistas know is that collectors are diverse and collecting is rather personal. But what we discover is that collecting is another way to connect to the Star Trek community. Hunting for items is like a quest and you can meet all sorts of people in the community willing to help you. Part of the unexpected fun of collecting is swapping stories of your quest for that item that completes your collection. And wow, does Jamie have great stories!

Here are the links we mention in the show:

https://exo-6.com/ Museum grade Star Trek Figurines

https://wixiban.com/ For all things about cards

https://fansets.com/collections/star-trek Our sponsor Fansets for all things about pins

You can find Mike and Jamie's cool podcast here: https://trekgeeks.com/thedivinetreasury

Send them a tweet and say the SyFy Sistas sent you: https://twitter.com/divine_treasury

We give a special shout out to our sound engineer DoS, the Anonymous. Find him at dostheanonymous1@gmail.com

You can find the SyFy Sistas online at syfysistas.com - Facebook @SyFy Sistas - Twitter: https://twitter.com/SyfySistas - Instagram: @syfy.sistas - And of course, www.trekgeeks.com/syfysistas
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: Moments Asunder with Dayton Ward
item.description:
SPOILER ALERT

It's the most epic Star Trek novel trilogy of all time!

This week, we welcome our old friend, Dayton Ward, author of Star Trek: Coda Book 1: Moments Asunder. Starfleet and the Federation face possibly their gravest threat yet.

We'll also discuss this trilogy concluding the novel continuity, and believe us there will be a lot of spoilers, so if you haven't read Moments Asunder yet, you're definitely going to want to!

STARFLEET'S FINEST FACES A CHALLENGE UNLIKE ANY OTHER

TOMORROW IS DOOMED Time is coming apart. Countless alternate and parallel realities are under attack, weakening and collapsing from relentless onslaught. If left unchecked, the universe faces an unstoppable descent toward entropy.

WANDERER, ORACLE, ALLY Scarred and broken after decades spent tracking this escalating temporal disaster, while battling the nameless enemy responsible for it, an old friend seeks assistance from Captain Jean-Luc Picard and the crew of the Starship Enterprise. The apocalypse may originate from their future, but might the cause lie in their past?

EVERYTHING THAT WILL BE Identifying their adversary is but the first step toward defeating them, but early triumphs come with dreadful costs. What will the price be to achieve final victory, and how will that success be measured in futures as yet undefined?
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: 023 - Black Representation in the Star Trek Movies
item.description:
This week the SyFy Sistas look at the thirteen Star Trek films to see how Black actors have fared in them. We rate the TOS crew, the TNG crew, and the Kelvin crew on inclusion. With the help of a repurposed Bechdel-Wallace type test (which gauges how women fare in films) that we call the SyFy Sistas Cultural Inclusion Checklist we hold these films up to their own IDIC imbued mirror. But it's not all "a day in diversity training", we select personal favorites (come on, it's got to be the one with the whales!), and reveal which scenes really get on our last nerves. Check out the Nerd Trek podcast #114 and #115 for J.D.'s full take on Star Trek: The Final Frontier.

Other podcasts mentioned in this show: Five by Five: A Movie Podcast (Subrina's other podcast) TrekMovie.com Shuttle Pod Podcast

We give a special shout out to our sound engineer DoS, the Anonymous. Find him at dostheanonymous1@gmail.com

You can find the SyFy Sistas online at syfysistas.com - Facebook @SyFySistas - Twitter: @SyfySistas - Instagram: @syfy.sistas - And of course, trekgeeks.com/syfysistas
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: 024 - Interview with the Andorian: Noah Averbach-Katz
item.description:
It happened in Vegas, and we are going to spill the tea. The SyFy Sistas have never laughed as hard as we did with everyone's favorite Discovery Season 3 cast member, Noah Averbach-Katz. The Son of Rachelle, while living a Trekkie's ultimate dream, earned his spot on the cast with his Julliard training, those super expressive eyes, and some good old-fashioned schmoozing. Noah gets silly and serious, and even makes a 'beta-canon logical' call on a burning question.

We give a special shout out to our sound engineer DoS, the Anonymous. Find him at dostheanonymous1@gmail.com or on Instagram @ dos_theanonymous_1

For more about all the fun we have in Star Fleet Command Quadrant One (SFCQ1) as the crew of the USS Gerard Holder, under the command of Lt. Yvette Blackmon Tom, go to: https://www.starfleet-command.com/ https://www.facebook.com/ussgerardholder

You can find the SyFy Sistas online at syfysistas.com - Facebook @SyFy Sistas - Twitter: @Syfy Sistas - Instagram: @syfy.sistas - And of course, trekgeeks.com/syfysistas
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: Doug Jones
item.description:
This week, we're joined by one of the most positive and joyful people in the Star Trek Universe!

This accomplished actor has been in so many memorable roles and, sometimes, we bet you didn't even know it was him! His ability to act while covered up under layers of latex and makeup make him one of the most talented human beings on the face of the Earth.

His character, Saru, on the highly acclaimed and award winning series, "Star Trek: Discovery" was the main focus of an article written by Bill which was published on StarTrek.com called "What I've Learned from Saru," which was also the focus of Trek Geeks #191.

We are honored to welcome the incredible Doug Jones to Trek Geeks for a discussion about his career, portraying Saru, and some of the other roles he's had which are among our favorites!
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: 025 - The Thanksgiving Show: Star Trek: Discovery Season 4 Episode 1
item.description:
It was late on a Thursday night and the Sistas were a little punchy. But never fear they perked up just enough to give you their opinions on Star Trek Discovery's latest episode "Kobayashi Maru". Although we were a bit distracted by the Grace Jones - like costume changes.

This is the Season 1 finale of the SyFy Sistas Podcasts. We will return on our First Year Anniversary (December 24th) with Episode 1 of Season 2.

A special thank you to Ryan T. Husk and Cirroc Lofton of the 7th Rule Podcast for always having our backs and promoting us (https://www.patreon.com/the7thrule). And to Bill and Dan of Trek Geeks for giving us that slot during Black History Month and offering us a spot on the roster. (https://www.trekgeeks.com/shows)

We give a special shout out to our sound engineer DoS, the Anonymous. Find him at dostheanonymous1@gmail.com or on Instagram @ dos_theanonymous_1

You can find the SyFy Sistas online at syfysistas.com - Facebook @SyFy Sistas - Twitter: @Syfy Sistas - Instagram: @syfy.sistas - And of course, www.trekgeeks.com/syfysistas
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: The Center Seat
item.description:
(This episode has been tagged as explicit for adult language.)

You'd think that after 55 years, we'd know just about everything about the making of Star Trek, right?

Guess again!

This week, we're joined by Brian Volk-Weiss--producer and director of a brand new docu-series airing on the History Channel, "The Center Seat: 55 Years of Star Trek." We'll discuss getting this 10 episode series made and hear some of the stories around its production--some of which haven't been heard very often in the last half century.

Plus, we'll hear about some of the items in Brian's collection and how his fandom started--and maybe he'll even give Bill a new reason to watch Star Trek: Generations?

Oh, and by the way, there MIGHT be a surprise giveaway this week--be sure to listen for details!
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: Oasis
item.description:
Star Trek: Enterprise paved a lot of ground in its first season, creating characters and a history of Starfleet. However, it also dared to bring a regular cast member from another Star Trek series on board as a guest star, playing a character completely unrelated.

We all know that the amazing René Auberjonois played Constable Odo for seven seasons on Star Trek: Deep Space Nine, but many people forget he also played Ezral in the first season Enterprise episode, "Oasis." For the second anniversary of his passing, we'll consider this episode and what René brings to it specifically.

Plus, we have a winner of some FanSets pins!

We've got one more brand-new episode in 2021 after this, so we hope you'll join us for this episode of The Flagship.
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: 2.01 - Ladies, Gentlemen, and Variations Thereupon, the One and Only Nana Visitor
item.description:
Who would have believed that a nonchalant fangirl post on the SyFy Sistas Muthaship Facebook group could lead to an actual squeal before letting the Fabulous One into the zoom room. The best Anniversary present ever!

Dr. Michelle Thaller - American Astronomer and research scientist for NASA Assistant Director for Science Communication at NASA's Goodard Space Flight Center in Maryland. Dr.Tara Brach - www.tarabrach.com - Psychologist, Author, Teacher, Meditation Jess Zimmerman - www.jesszimmerman.com - "Women and Other Monsters" Nana Visitor aka "Mama Nana" on Instagram: @visitornana The Star Trek Cruise - www.startrekthecruise.com Rosetta Y. Burke, Major General (retired)

A special thank you to Bill and Dan from Trek Geeks for getting us in touch with Ms. Visitor.

This is our First Year Anniversary (December 24th) show Season 2; Episode 1

We give a special shout out to our sound engineer DoS, the Anonymous. Find him at dostheanonymous1@gmail.com or on Instagram @ dos_theanonymous_1

You can find the SyFy Sistas online at syfysistas.com - Facebook @SyFy Sistas - Twitter: @Syfy Sistas - Instagram: @syfy.sistas - And of course, trekgeeks.com/syfysistas
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: 2.02 - Critical Race Theory in Star Trek with Dr. Crystal Fleming
item.description:
The SyFy Sistas sit down with Dr. Crystal Fleming, a critical race sociologist, author of three books and an internationally recognized expert on racism and antiracism. Her work empowers people of all backgrounds to become change agents and dismantle white supremacy. Our conversation ranges from Black Respectability politics to the myth of Utopia in the Star Trek universe, to our deep love of Deep Space Nine and Captain Benjamin Lafayette Sisko.

Her books include:

  • Resurrecting Slavery: Racial Legacies and White Supremacy in France
  • How to Be Less Stupid About Race: On Racism, White Supremacy, and the Racial Divide
  • RISE UP! How You Can Join the Fight Against White Supremacy

You can follow Dr. Fleming at https://twitter.com/alwaystheself

========================================= Visit our sponsor Aw Yeah Comics here: www.awyeahcomics.com

We give a special shout out to our sound engineer DoS, the Anonymous. Find him at Dostheanonymous1@gmail.com and on IG: @dos_theanonymous_1

Find the SyFy Sistas online at syfysistas.com - Facebook: @SyFySistas - Twitter: @SyFy Sistas - IG: syfy.sistas - And of course, at trekgeeks.com/syfysistas
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: The BIG Sci-fi Podcast Announcement!
item.description: We're excited to announce our brand new podcast!  Sci-fi fans from all over North America have united for this fun and light-hearted opinion-based podcast.  We love Trek the most, but our love for Trek has given us a deep love for all things sci-fi.  Coming February 4th to a podcast universe near you!
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: 2.03 - Dr. Mohamed Noor: The Bio Trekkie Explains EVERYTHING!
item.description:
It's 'Science with Mohamed' as the Sistas get the answers to those questions you've been dying to ask. The episodes "Distant Origin" and "Lineage" from Voyager, and "Masterpiece Society" and "Up the Long Ladder" from TNG come up in this conversation. But as we delve into that whale movie and any season of Discovery it becomes science 2.0. Dr. Noor debunks a Sista's "in the year 2525" theory, explains the difference between selective breeding and genetic engineering, and confirms that if there one thing we never have to worry about it's an attack of giant ants.

For more on Dr. Mohamed Noor go to: https://scholars.duke.edu/person/noor

Read his really cool book: https://press.princeton.edu/books/hardcover/9780691177410/live-long-and-evolve

Watch his really cool show Bio Trekkie Explains: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC5zvlD5RnkzhIegG0gnSeSw

And follow him on The Twitter: https://twitter.com/mafnoor

We give a special shout out to our sound engineer DoS, the Anonymous. Find him at dostheanonymous1@gmail.com

You can find the SyFy Sistas online at syfysistas.com Facebook @SyFy Sistas Twitter: https://twitter.com/SyfySistas Instagram: @syfy.sistas And of course, www.trekgeeks.com/syfysistas
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: TREK*talks with Jeri Ryan & John Billingsley
item.description:
This week, we bring you the audio from our conversation with Star Trek: Voyager & Star Trek: Picard's Jeri Ryan! Then following that, we have a special TREK*talks follow-up segment with John Billingsley to talk about the telethon and plans for next year!

Plus, tell you about everything else that's been going on in the lives of the Trek Geeks as we celebrate our 7th anniversary on this installment of The Flagship!

SUPPORT THE HOLLYWOOD FOOD COALITION

If you didn't get a chance to check out TREK*talks, you can still watch it on our YouTube channel--and you can also make your tax deductible donation to the Hollywood Food Coalition today at https://donate.trektalks.net!
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: The Mission and Crew
item.description:
This inaugural episode introduces our hosts as we talk about ourselves and our favorites inside and outside of Trek. The discussion is fun and entertaining as we go all around the sci-fi universe in describing our fandom. We also talk about TNG and DS9's portrayal of Worf's parenting style. In addition, we learn of an incredible sci-fi series of the past from the self-proclaimed "old guy" of our crew.  

Hosts:

item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: 2.04 - Far Beyond the Stars 24th Anniversary Tribute
item.description:
Far Beyond the Stars, one of the most celebrated episodes in the Star Trek franchise, was written by one of the most creative, thought-provoking, and prolific tv science fiction writers of our time, Marc Scott Zicree. The Sistas sat down with Marc to talk about how the then controversial Deep Space Nine episode made it to the screen, why Avery Brooks had to direct it, and the efforts of the cast and crew to make it as authentic as possible. But Marc told us we could ask him anything, so we touched on the state of science fiction tv today, writing for tv verses films, and what's coming out of his very own production studio.

For more on Marc Scott Zicree (and there's a lot!) go to his YouTube Channel: https://www.youtube.com/c/MrSciFi/community

His very cool series Space Command https://spacecommand-theseries.com/

And follow him on The Twitter: https://twitter.com/MarcZicree

We give a special shout out to our sound engineer DoS, the Anonymous. Find him at dostheanonymous1@gmail.com

You can find the SyFy Sistas online at syfysistas.com Facebook @SyFy Sistas Twitter: twitter.com/SyfySistas Instagram: @syfy.sistas And of course, trekgeeks.com/syfysistas
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: Armin Shimerman
item.description:
This week, Bill & Dan are honored to welcome a very special guest to the podcast.

When Trek Geeks began seven years ago, this person was one of the first we put on our "wish list" of guests--those we really hoped to be able to chat with some day.

Armin Shimerman from Star Trek: Deep Space Nine joins The Flagship to discuss his time on Trek, his novels, his involvement with the Pancreatic Cancer Action Network (PanCAN) and why that charity means so much.

This is a discussion you won't want to miss!

Plus, Dan wants you to write a Farkism for him--be sure to listen for details!

PanCAN PURPLE STRIDE! Help Bill & Dan raise money for the PanCAN Purple Stride walk in Los Angeles. If they can raise $2,500.00, they'll fly out to Los Angeles to walk in the event with Kitty Swink!

https://trekgeeks.com/pancan

READ ARMIN'S NOVELS Check out Armin's novels in the Illyria series from his publisher, Jumpmaster Press!

https://www.jumpmasterpress.com/product-page/illyria-series
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: Robots and AI in Sci-Fi
item.description:
Our very own robot expert and sci-fi author, Adeena, leads us through an interesting discussion about our favorite robots/AI from around the sci-fi universe!  We focus on a fascinating episode of The Outer Limits called "I, Robot."  We really nerd out on robots in this episode and you might just be surprised at what we discuss!  Join us for this 2nd episode and leave us a comment via email: bigscifipodcast@gmail.com.

item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: A Long Time Ago...Star Wars.
item.description:
This week we dive into the Star Wars universe and discuss whether or not it is true sci-fi.  Steve leads us through hyperspace as we get a little lively about our feelings positive and negative towards this incredibly deep and rich franchise in entertainment.  You'll hear some great stories and debate as a bunch of Star Trek fans, who happen to be Star Wars fans as well, talk about the rival of our beloved Trek!  You'll laugh and reminisce with us as we have fun being together again to talk sci-fi.


Email us your thoughts and comments - we may even mention you on a future episode: bigscifipodcast@gmail.com.

item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: SyFy Sistas
item.description:
Last year at this time, we invited a relatively new podcast called SyFy Sistas to take over the Trek Geeks Podcast for an episode during Black History Month. They wowed us with their passion and they spoke to our hearts with their positivity.

Shortly after that they became part of the Trek Geeks family and we celebrate that--and them--today as Bill and Dan welcome Tamia, Yvette, and Subrina to The Flagship to discuss their fandom, how the podcast started, the concept of Afrofuturism, and what they hope to do next!

Plus, did you know you could write a Farkism for Dan and possibly win a $50 gift card to FanSets? YUP! It's all in this week's episode!
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: Do the Ends Justify the Means? An Ethical Discussion of Trek.
item.description:
Cristian Fox leads this week's discussion surrounding the very important question of whether the ends ever justifies the means, especially when thinking about the Star Trek universe.  We discuss several episodes from Enterprise, Deep Space Nine, and Voyager in this week's episode.  This discussion gets serious, but we hope you have fun listening and engaging with this week's discussion because we are four of the most passionate Trek fans around and we have opinions!

NOTE: This episode was recorded prior to the Russian incursion into Ukraine.  We talk heavily about war in this episode and even mention Russia by name.  We make comparisons based on the stories in Trek of association for the sake of our discussion.  We are releasing this episode with great sensitivity to what is happening in Europe and would like to simply state that we, as a podcasting team, are against the invasion of a sovereign people over another.

Email us your thoughts and comments - we may even mention you on a future episode: bigscifipodcast@gmail.com.

Buy Adeena's book here: https://www.amazon.com/dp/B0951DLMPF

item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: 2.05 - The Many Faces of David Benjamin Tomlinson
item.description: David Benjamin Tomlinson, the alter ego of Star Trek Discovery's favorite Saurian science officer, Linus, joins us for a wide-ranging conversation. The talented writer, producer, and actor spills the tea on keeping up as a Klingon, his cast buddies, and how he made Sonequa Martin-Green crack up his first day on the set as Linus.
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: The Trauma of Picard - The Cost of Leadership
item.description:
Our fantastic crew dives deep into Trek in this week's episode and get deep in our discussion over the trauma that Picard experiences throughout the run of TNG on television and on the big screen. We also talk about the cost of leading affectively and what that looks like. This is, perhaps, the most personal episode yet as we share who influences us in our own lives.

If you like what you hear and enjoy this podcast, we kindly and humbly ask that you share it on your social media and help others join in the fun and fandom!

Email us your thoughts and comments - we may even mention you on a future episode: bigscifipodcast@gmail.com.

item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: It's Only a Paper Moon
item.description:
This week, Bill & Dan look at the standout Season 7 episode of Star Trek: Deep Space Nine, "It's Only a Paper Moon!"

Nog returns to DS9 after losing his leg in the events of "The Siege of AR-558" in an effort to continue his recovery. Starfleet has put him on medical leave, but Nog wants nothing to do with the real world. He escapes into the holosuites and the Vic Fontaine program--but will he ever leave?

Aron Eisenberg truly shines in this episode, and we'll talk about all the reasons why. Plus, we'll look at Nog's escapism and compare it to how many people--including us--have dealt with the pandemic the last couple of years.

This is one you're not gonna want to miss, pally.
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: Humor in Sci-Fi
item.description:
This week's episode will have you slapping your knee and laughing out loud as our crew discusses how humor is used across the science fiction universe.  We talk about our favorite films and television shows, our favorite moments of humor in sci-fi, and work through a checklist of sci-fi films and shows that fans might love to add to their own watchlists! Join us as we joke around and talk seriously about what makes us laugh in the sci-fi universe.

Email us your thoughts and comments - we may even mention you on a future episode: bigscifipodcast@gmail.com.

item.title: Time Travel
item.description:
Steve Mirkin takes us on a mysterious journey through time and space in The BIG Sci-Fi Zone! This week, we are talking about time travel. Do we love it or hate it? What are our favorite time travel films and stories? This week's podcast episode is a lot of fun and, quite possibly, the most interesting yet! Join us, if you dare, on a fantastic voyage into the future...or the past...you know what we mean! 


Email us your thoughts and comments - we may even mention you on a future episode: bigscifipodcast@gmail.com.

item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: Newsapalooza
item.description:
With all the craziness in our lives, it's been a while since we've been able to talk about some of the amazing news we've had in the Star Trek Universe the past couple of months. In this episode, we attempt to rectify that gap in your podcast listening!

We'll discuss a bunch of major Trek stories including Kelvin Timeline movies, action figures, casting news, Mission Chicago updates and so much more! Plus, Dan announces the winner of the Farkism contest and reads the best Farkism this podcast has ever had!

(Audio Note: Some of Bill's audio in parts of the podcast has quality issues in this episode due to an issue with our recording platform. We apologize that his mellifluous tones aren't as mellifluous as they normally are. Luckily, his face is still glorious. Yes, Bill wrote this.)
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: Diversity and Inclusion - Star Wars vs. Star Trek
item.description:
Cris Fox leads us this week into a deep and relevant topic on adversity and inclusion in both Star Wars and Star Trek.  Which of these franchises handles these issues the best, and what can we learn from their examples.  We keep it real, but we also have fun and poke fun at these two fantastically entertaining sci-fi franchises.  Revealing and entertaining conversation is ahead for those brave enough to face the final frontier of the human experience!

Email us your thoughts and comments - we may even mention you on a future episode: bigscifipodcast@gmail.com.

item.title: Forbidden Planet
item.description:
Brian leads us where we have never gone before...the Forbidden Planet!  We discuss what's so "forbidden" about it and genuinely have fun picking apart this classic sci-fi film.  We have plenty of laughs along the way, but this episode is so interesting and fascinating that even a Vulcan mind-meld would reveal that the BIG Sci-Fi Podcast crew is definitely worth listening to!  

Music featured at the opening of this episode was written by Louis and Bebe Barron and is featured on the Forbidden Planet soundtrack.

item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: 2.06 - Star Trek: The Cruise V Convo
item.description:
It's been a while, so the SyFy Sistas present our 12" Disco Version of Star Trek: The Cruise V.

This is our love letter to Royal Caribbean Cruises and Entertainment Cruise Productions, some of the hardest working people at sea. But we aren't the only ones telling sea stories.

We taped a few of our new friends while on board for a real "And You Were There" feel.
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: Mission Chicago Preview
item.description:
It's been over two years since there's been an official Star Trek Convention and, this weekend, we gather again to celebrate the franchise we love!

Bill and Dan are headed to Star Trek: Mission Chicago and, this week on the Flagship, they look at this new convention experience looks like. From panels to vendors, Bill and Dan break it down. They'll also talk about what they hope to see from their weekend on Chicago's South Side.
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: Virtual Reality!
item.description:
Adeena takes us into the brave new world of VR as we discuss the sci-fi we love most with this concept and "reality" as part of the story telling.  We get deep and, at times, not so deep on this topic as it relates to our discussion.  Whether we're thinking deeply or just having fun as we talk about VR, you will definitely have fun listening to this interesting and engaging discussion.  Grab your favorite beverage and snack, pull up a chair, and join us as we enjoy the company of friends once again on The BIG Sci-Fi Podcast!

Contact us with comments, ideas, and compliments: bigscifipodcast@gmail.com

item.title: Tommy Kraft Interview - Star Trek: Horizon
item.description:
Our crew had the pleasure of welcoming aboard the podcast filmmaker, Tommy Kraft, who masterfully helmed the fan film project, Star Trek: Horizon.  He is an exciting young talent in the film industry and he was just in college when he wrote, directed, produced, composed the music score, made the costumes, and the list goes on!  We had a wonderful conversation about the making of this incredibly impressive fan film.  We learned a lot about the process of making a film and it is fascinating, as Mr. Spock would say!  Join us for our interesting and entertaining interview of Mr. Tommy Kraft.

For help understanding, to raise awareness of, or to receive help if you suffer from OCD, check out this helpful and informational website: 

https://centerforanxietydisorders.com/what-is-harm-ocd/


The crew of The BIG Sci-Fi Podcast are grateful to Tommy for being so transparent and open about his struggles while making this film.  OCD is a real disorder and effects many people everyday.  We are proud to raise awareness of this disorder as we are inspired by Tommy's courage to push through it while making this film and beyond.  We hope to offer hope and help to others who might hear Tommy's story of perseverance and reach out for the help they need as well. 


Be sure to share this podcast on social media with all of your fellow lovers of sci-fi!

item.title: Men In Black
item.description:
Cris Fox introduces us to the Men In Black and we do our best to remember the sequel films (even though they aimed that pencil looking brain washing thingy at us)! This episode has a lot of laughs in it as we talk about the hilarious and unique Men In Black films. Cris wants to know why the original film was so great and the following three were hit or miss, depending on your perspective. Make sure you're suited up and ready for anything as we invade your entertainment space this week! You won't want to forget about this episode so make sure not to look at the pencil looking brain washing thingy!

item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: My Kind of Town?: Mission Chicago Wrap-Up
item.description:
Thanks to a global pandemic, it's been nearly three years since there has been an Official Star Trek Convention to attend.

That all changed a few weeks ago when ReedPOP--the new licensee for Star Trek conventions--held their first event in Chicago, Illinois! Star Trek: Mission Chicago saw fans of all ages return to the convention scene to celebrate the franchise we love, and it wasn't without its issues.

Bill and Dan will be joined by Ron Wrobel, host of the brand-new podcast "Con Pod: A Star Trek Conventions Podcast," to talk about the good, the bad, and the disappointing with this first travelling show.

They'll be looking at the categories of Schedule, Vendors, Celeb Experience, and Overall Experience and assigning a grade to each. How will they rate the convention? Listen now and find out!
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks

item.title: The Music of Science Fiction Films
item.description:
Brian leads our symphony of voices this week as we discuss the wonderful music that is often found in the science fiction films we love and, perhaps, don't love so much.  We laugh a lot in this episode and our tangents are epic this week!  You'll also learn about the eclectic tastes in music represented in our crew, as well as which franchise we would write a music score for, if we suddenly had the ability to do so!  This episode crescendos on high note so you don't want to miss a single beat!  Sit back with your favorite beverage and turn the volume up as this week's episode is a headbanger for sure!

item.title: MST3K: Manos, The Hands of Fate - w/ Special Guest, Jackey Neyman Jones
item.description:
Jackey Neyman Jones joins us for this week's conversation as we talk about one of the most popular episodes of Mystery Science Theater: 3K which covered the 1966 film, Manos the Hands of Fate. Jackey plays the little girl in the film, Debbie. Her father, Tom Neyman, played the master in the film and was an accomplished stage actor.  Besides being one of the most popular and hilarious episodes of MST3K, this film is also considered to be one of the worst films of all time. We had the privilege to sit down with Jackey to talk about the story of Manos, her father, MST3K, her experience while filming Manos, as well as what she has been up to in recent years. Jackey is a talented artist and is producing amazing artwork and is a part of a new independent production that is seeking to make a series based on Manos (see link below). This is a fascinating look into a strange and fun part of film history. We were delighted to talk with Jackey and you will be delighted to hear her stories and how you can actually connect with her and her artwork.


Buy Jackey's book about her experience on Manos!

Subscribe to Jackey's YouTube page!

Sign up for Jackey's art class!

Join her Facebook fan page!

Watch the MST3K Episode featuring Manos, the Hands of Fate!

Watch the trailer for The Manos Chronicles!

item.title: 2.07 - Meet Jayne Brook
item.description:
The SyFy Sistas sit down with Jayne Brook, a film and TV actor that has earned three Screen Actors Guild nominations for Chicago Hope and a Prism Award for Performance in a Drama Series for Boston Legal. We know her for her role as Star Trek Discovery's Vice Admiral Katrina Cornwell. This role finally redeems StarFleet Admirals in every Star Trek "universe" and/or "timeline". Our conversation ranges from underwear fittings, the Garage Door explanation, the epic tweet, SMG, Bio Trekkies, being a DS9 newbie, white Jesus, scattered Black rocks and Jayne's oh-so detailed research of the SyFy Sistas.

For more on Jayne Brook check out her IMDb page: https://www.imdb.com/name/nm0111632/

item.title: Doug Drexler, Part 1: Academy Award Winner and 2 Time Emmy Winner
item.description:
Doug Drexler is a life-long Star Trek fan and has been a part of all the Berman era Star Trek shows, the TNG movies, and Picard. He won an Academy Award for his make-up work on the film Dick Tracy and two Emmy's for his special effect work on Battlestar Galactica. Our crew was honored to have Mr. Drexler, who is a true Star Trek expert and passionate fan as a part of our podcast.  His knowledge is vast and his hands on experience with the shows and films is extraordinary.  He truly cares about this franchise and you will hear it in his voice as he describes his life and experience with Trek.  Our conversation with Doug was so great we needed to break it into two parts.  

Explore Doug's work over the years!

To contact us: bigscifipodcast@gmail.com

item.title: Strange New Worlds
item.description:
SPOILER ALERT!

This week, Bill and Dan look at the debut of the latest live action Star Trek series, "Strange New Worlds!" Normally this is a topic reserved for Discovering Trek, but with all of the acclaim the series has received from fans and critics alike, they couldn't help but talk about it on this installment of The Flagship!

In addition to talking about the characters, they'll also discuss highlights from the series' first two episodes, "Strange New Worlds," and "Children of the Comet."

If you HAVEN'T watched Star Trek: Strange New Worlds yet, you might want to wait until you do before you dive in on this episode!

Plus, news about FanGeeks Party 2022 in Fabulous Las Vegas, Nevada that you're not going to want to miss!

item.description:

We got to do something we never dreamed we'd do and had to share it with you. We'd like to thank Trek Geeks Bill and Dan (Hi, Ted!) for getting us on the screeners and junket lists, which starting this whole incredible thing. We'd also like to acknowledge Mackenzie who helped us on interview day and got us that spot on the Red Carpet, and Leigh for helping us at the theater and later at the V.I.P. Party (thanks for letting us give Anson our tee shirt).

item.title: The Search for Spock
item.description:
Dan and Bill return to The Flagship this week to discuss the third Star Trek film--the feature film directorial debut of Leonard Nimoy, "Star Trek III: The Search for Spock!"

This is a movie that the Trek Geeks have been split on over the years and, in this episode, they'll discuss why.

From casting, to script, to memorable scenes and what may just be William Shatner's finest performance in Star Trek, they'll cover it all this week on Trek Geeks!

SPOILERS AHEAD!" We discuss all kinds of stuff from the series so be warned - we talk about a lot of stuff! item.title: The "Problem" with Discovery
item.description:
Back in May, Bill wrote an op-ed style piece for TrekNews.net titled, "Star Trek: Discovery Has Problems (& How They Can Be Fixed)," which was a constructive look at the issues many fans feel that Discovery has as far as crisis fatigue.

It was an article that caused a lot of discussion and, this week, Bill and Dan return to break down the points the article makes and discuss how this enormously successful show might improve.

item.title: 2.11 - Introducing Your New Pilot: Melissa Navia!
item.description:
WARNING this episode of the SyFy Sistas Podcast is full of SPOILERS! If you have not watched season 1 of Star Trek: Strange New Worlds please head to your Paramount+ streaming service and watch it now.

The Sistas met Melissa in April and the assignment was to ask questions about this great new Star Trek Show but in the end; we bonded over short hair, the authentic portrayal of military women and of course, our beloved USS Enterprise's newest pilot, Lt. Erica Ortegas.

It is July, the first Season is over. Join us as we get to know Melissa Navia the actor, the widow, and the cool aunt with a fiercely innate penchant for short hair.

Find out more about Melissa Navia: https://melissanavia.zenfolio.com/ https://linktr.ee/mcnavia Girl With Short Hair: www.girlwithshorthair.com

This Changes Everything: https://www.imdb.com/title/tt5795282/

You can support the SyFy Sistas podcast on Patreon at: https://www.patreon.com/syfysistas Thank you Dena Massenburg for our dope logo: @blackbeanz70 Thank you to our sound engineer DoS, the Anonymous: @dos_theanonymous_1 FAN SETS - our pins...have character We want to thank our friends at FanSets for being the presenting sponsor of the Trek Geeks Podcast Network. www.fansets.com ALL THE SYFY SISTAS INFO AT YOUR FINGER TIPS : https://linktr.ee/syfysistas

item.title: Galaxy Quest!
item.description:
The always friendly and always encouraging, Steve, takes us on a Galaxy Quest to discuss one of the funniest and most endearing science fiction movies ever made!  Galaxy Quest made fun of everything in sci-fi fandom, especially Star Trek, but did so in a way that honored fans and their love of Trek.  If you haven't watched this film, you will want to after listening to Adeena, Brian, Cris, and Steve talk about it!  Never give up, and never surrender!

This podcast is a part of the Listen Frederick Podcast Network!
